Tag Archives: mr. Bean

You may recall the great British comedy series “Mr. Bean” from your childhood. In those days, “Mr. Bean” was in heavy rotation on the Cartoon Network, back when it was a relatively new channel. At the time, I thought the show was pretty clever. In my mind’s eye, I can still see the opening credits, which featured an animated sequence of Mr. Bean going about his daily routine, which inevitably ended with him getting himself into some sort of predicament.

The show revolved around Mr. Bean, a seemingly intelligent Everyman who got himself into some sort of problem almost every episode. In typical “Saturday morning TV” style, the solutions to Mr. Bean’s problems were either ludicrous or downright preposterous, but they were always funny to me as a child. I was quite smitten by this ordinary man who accomplished extraordinary things, and despite his shortcomings, I believed in him.

As time passed and I watched the show with slightly more cynical eyes, perhaps even a touch of cynicism is deserved by the ending scenes of each episode. In one episode, for example, Bean

middle earth map

Legend has it that one night Gandalf the Grey walked out into the Dwarf-city of Moria and, as he leaned on the gate, it collapsed, swallowing him. This site is all about the setting and history of J.R.R. Tolkien's Middle-earth, and the works that inspired the creation of it, including the author's own legendarium, or fantasy history of the world. I hope you enjoy reading about it here as much as I have enjoying creating it. I've got several maps of Middle Earth here, including an official (but not endorsed by Tolkien) The One Ring world map of Middle-earth, as well as a more detailed map showing the lands of Gondor and Middle-earth, with some parts of Beleriand still undecided. And if that's not enough, you can also check out a map of all of Tolkien's published works!

I hope you enjoy your visit and please feel free to leave comments.

The World As It Is Right Now

Recently I had an experience that made me think of what the future might hold. I had an opportunity to travel to a different part of the world. As a frequent flyer, this was a trip I had saved and wanted to take for sometime. I booked my trip, did my packing and was all set to enjoy my time away from the usual hustle and bustle of life. My only problem was that my family was coming to India with me. This meant I’d be flying with two heavy bags and one hand carry bag. I wasn’t too thrilled with this idea as I didn’t want to overload the plane. After some thought, I decided to keep only my hand carry bag and leave the heavy bags at the hotel. I had already booked my return flight so I’d have to rush back to the airport in plenty of time. On our way to the airport, the boys and I had a discussion about our travel experience. As expected, my son came with lots of questions. He asked why didn’t we fly business class and why our tickets were refundable. In response, I tried to explain the rules about

We are happy to report that after 3 years of development, our first-generation DLTK (Decentralized Local Trading Protocol) stable coin KRWDT (Korean Won-backed Digital Token) is finally functional on the ICONOMI platform. You can now buy, sell, insure, and withdrawal KRWDT from our Singapore dollar-pegged stablecoin wallet.

The purpose of the KRWDT stablecoin is to provide a 1:1 stablecoin pegged 1:1 to the Korean Won, with the stability that comes from being fully collateralized by a basket of Korean Won. As ICONOMI users have been asking us to support Korean Won-pegged stablecoins for long time, we are very happy to deliver this first product. As always, the road towards stablecoin stabilization is not easy, and we will keep you updated on our progress.

Follow our news on Telegram, Twitter, and Facebook to stay up to date on all things ICONOMI. Please continue to send us your feedback, as we are continuously refining our product to better serve you.

ICONOMI product team

civil rights activist Malcolm X once asked, “What does it mean to be a n—–?” Malcolm X described the meaning of the n-word in racial terms, as a derogatory term for a black person. That is, the n-word is a racial slur. The terms “racist” and “racial slur” are often used interchangeably, but they have different legal connotations and represent different ways of thinking about our society.

The Dictionary.com defines a racial slur as “a harsh word or phrases applied to a person of another race, especially one considered insulting or derogatory.”1 The N-word is a racial slur in common parlance and as such is a word that is absolutely never appropriate for use in polite company. While some may argue that it is not a racial slur, the term has a clear racist implication. Additionally, the word is considered vulgar, profane, and a bad word. According to Oxford Dictionaries, a racial slur is a “harsh word or phrases applied to a person of another race that is considered insulting or derogatory.”2 This is clearly the case when referring to people of another race. The same cannot be said for “racist,”

midwives - women who deliver babies. It's not uncommon for a woman to give birth using a midwife. It's a natural and normal thing to do. Many families like to have a midwife present during labor and actually have the baby delivered by the midwife. midwives attend births, and if needed, they can provide emergency assistance during childbirth. Some midwives are nurse-midwives, which means they have additional training in delivering babies. Nurse-midwives usually provide services in a hospital, but they can also offer services at another setting if they have a cooperating agreement with the hospital. Another speciality is home birth, where the mother chooses to give birth at her own home. This is becoming more popular due to the concern about medical mistakes and the increasing cesarean rate. Some people see nothing wrong with giving birth at home or using a midwife. This also has become more popular. The American Congress of Obstetricians and Gynecologists has warned that midwives do not provide safe care for babies and mothers, and do not have the ability to practice normal childbirth. They encourage all pregnant women to deliver in a physician-run hospital. As more and more women are having babies at home and using midwives, the American Congress

mid range crossbow ? Yes No Probably not Tell me why !

I am pretty sure that your answer depends on how much you are into archery.

If you are an occasional archer who enjoys going out into the woods and try your hand at shotting a wild turkey, your answer would most likely be “yes”!

If you are an avid archer who owns multiple compound and long range crossbows, your answer would most likely be “no” because you are likely in the mid range crossbow category.

What is a mid range crossbow?

A mid range crossbow is typically a long range crossbow in between a compound and a long range crossbow in terms of distance.

The range of a mid range crossbow is between 250 yards and 2000 yards. Unlike long range crossbows that take around 4-10 minutes to charge, mid range crossbows take around 1 hour to an entire day to fully charge.

I would classify the average archer who owns a mid range crossbow as someone who enjoys target shooting and plinking at cans, bottles, and various other common archery targets in addition to hunting.

What is quinoa?

Quinoa is a small grain that has been around for thousands of years. The Incan Empire even used quinoa as money due to its high value. Despite being a seed, it’s very soft and fluffy, so it’s perfect for use in soups and desserts.

In this recipe, you’ll cook quinoa in vegetable or chicken broth, depending on what you prefer. I prefer the flavor of vegetable broth, but either will work fine in this recipe.

"the end is nigh" meme started by the US government is not about doomsday prepping it's actually about budget cuts



The Bible tells us in the book of Revelations that there will be trumpets, signs, and wars before the "great and terrible day of God" aka Jesus Christ's second coming to earth.

One thing is for sure, in the times we live in now, you would have to be stupid to not being practicing some kind of prepping. For the average person survivalism is just common sense. You might consider yourself a prepper but chances are you shop bought food, wear regular clothing, and live in a house, not a underground bunker. Most preppers realize that being ready for disaster doesn't mean you have money or stuff to burn a shelter with and so the "doomsday prepping" mindset has evolved into more of a "general disaster preparedness" mindset.



Recently, a movement on Twitter called the "the end is nigh" meme started by the US government is actually about budget cuts and not about doomsday prepping. You can read more about it in this article from Vice Magazine:

A simple method you can use to protect your privacy is to use Private Browsing mode in your web browser. This will ensure that third parties cannot track your activity on the web and your online browsing activity will not be saved.

We also highly recommend using a Virtual Private Network (VPN) which will anonymize your online activity by routing all your internet traffic through a cloud server that is located in a region with less stringent internet regulations (that’s essentially the same as using Private Browsing mode).

Fun & Learning facts, figures and trivia


The most recorded artists in music history are The Beatles. There are over 20,000 songs recorded by The Beatles. Their first song was "Chasing Cigarettes" in 1962. Ringo Starr was the last member of The Beatles to leave the band in 1970. The Beatles Anthology I & II combined contain every song ever recorded by The Beatles. There are 4.555 kilometers of cable used to produce the 20,000+ records made by The Beatles. Paul McCartney is the only original member of The Beatles to appear in the Super Bowl. While in Japan in 2009, McCartney appeared in a TV advertisement for Japanese carmaker Nissan. He performed the song "Yes" from the album Kisses in the Dark with fellow Nissan client Jordan. It was also revealed that McCartney wrote a number of the band's songs while on loan from the Japanese edition of the "Queen" soundtrack. All 14 original actors from West Side Story have died.

result in the range of 65% up to 70%, but this is not an uncommon failure rate for IVF. This means a quarter of couples who try to conceive with IVF are not successful. If you are struggling to become pregnant after trying for over a year without success, see if you qualify for donor eggs or an egg freezing strategy. There are several states that permit donor eggs to be carried through to pregnancy, so it is definitely worth checking your local rules. Egg freezing is an easy and effective option to try later on if you do not become pregnant with your own eggs. If you have not done so already, it is also a good idea to review your IVF treatment strategy and procedures with a fertility specialist. This may be an issue where you are not making optimal use of your IVF resources or have gaps in your treatment plan that you could close. In some cases, your clinic may be able to help identify ways to improve your chances of success.

What is IVF?

In vitro fertilization (IVF) is a treatment for couples who are unable to conceive naturally within a year. This is known as 'ovulation failure.' The conventional treatment has a 'clinical pregnancy' rate of 25% to 40%. This means for every four

"I, Tonya" Is a Real Heartbreaker

The movie might win Best Performance by an Actress in a Drama at the Oscars this Sunday—but that’s not what makes it so remarkable

It may be shocking to learn that, even with its positive portrayal of a female hockey player overcoming great obstacles to achieve success, I, Tonya is very much a Hollywood he-man movie.

It features a who’s who of male thespians—such as Gary Oldman, Kyle Chandler, and Sasha Baron Cohen—playing characters with despicable alpha-male qualities. Sure, there are a few token women in supporting roles (such as Brendan Fraser and Susan Misner), and Michelle Yeoh shows up for no particular reason. But when it comes to the leads, the movie is a testosterone-soaked showcase for one of the all-time great action movie bad guys, Jean-Claude Van Damme.

Lifelong learning for the new global workforce

This paper, based on research carried out in partnership between UNI and Kingston University, discusses policy recommendations intended to improve the support new and experienced workers receive to develop workplace leadership skills and become more resilient and flexible in the face of changing jobs and career directions.

The paper looks at current trends in the change in work patterns, such as job polarisation, increasing number of part-time and temporary jobs, decreasing job security and skills gaps, and the rise of passive job-seekers. The paper then considers the case for investing in lifelong learning to better equip workers for the jobs of the future, recognising that individuals are more likely to have several jobs in their lives than in the past.

New and experienced workers alike require different skills for the jobs of the future. New workers often require technical skills, but are less likely to have industry-specific experience and need to be able to learn quickly, whereas experienced workers often have industry experience and managerial experience, but need to develop specific technical skills or knowledge, and be able to operate in a more creative role.

As a result of these differences in skills, workers will need to be more resilient and flexible in the face of changing jobs and careers,

Lifestream, the social network for streaming your life on the internet since 2012, has announced it is shutting down its service. The company said in a Facebook post Friday that it will delete its servers on April 15, 2023.


Lifestream was founded in 2012 by CEO Renée Di Rosa, former CEO of Hulu and former president of Sony Pictures Animation. Lifestream allowed you to create a web page to stream videos from all your social media accounts—like Instagram, Facebook, and Vimeo—and save them so you didn't have to repeatedly upload them. The company raised $20 million in funding from investors like DST Global and Index Ventures and expanded to also include podcasts and radio stations.

However, Di Rosa told TechCrunch in a statement that the company was "never able to monetize the platform nor the user growth we achieved." She added that while they had considered "a number of path for the business, all of them required taking on more external investment andscaling the team beyond whatwas possible."


Lifestream wasn't the only attempt to make the social web easier to use for streaming video, but it, along with rivals like Dicta, was one of the more popular services in

"You should have seen this coming."

Facing my ex-boyfriend in my old living room, tears streaming down my face, was not part of my plan. Not at all. But there we were, an awkward family photo moment later, and I was alone in my apartment with my thoughts.

The summer before I started college, we met every weekend at our favorite hiking spot. He'd take a different route to class every day, and I'd follow -- sometimes I'd stay home and read, other days I'd talk to him over the mountain. I couldn't get enough of his stories from the city, about artists he thought I'd like, restaurants I should try, places we could move once we graduated. And, just like every other girl, I swooned.

But something was off. After a few months, I started to realize I'd never fully heard his stories the way I wanted.

It all started back in 2002. My father Roy, mother Mary and my two twin sisters Margaret and Alex were driving home from church when they noticed something strange down by the shore. It was a crisp Autumn morning and the shores of Loch Ness were usually deserted. But this was none ordinary. There, floating just below the surface, was a creature unlike any they had ever seen. A beast with a long neck, humpbacked and with an enormous powerful tail.

"Stop the van!" I can clearly remember my mother crying.

As they pulled over, the creature slowly opened it's eyes and looked up at them. My father took a video camera and started filming.

"Holy crap, she's real!" my mother exclaimed.

Lifetime average mass and radius for white dwarfs from the SDSS-I/SEGUE data.
We present the first results from the Sloan Extranet Galaxy Evolution Experiment (SEGUE) project, which aims to identify and characterize Milky Way white dwarfs. We use the SEGUE Stellar Parameter Pipeline to determine the mass and radius for white dwarfs identified in the SDSS stripe 82, with additional followup spectroscopy for 8 candidates with unusually large radii. As a result, we obtain the average mass and radius for white dwarfs as a function of temperature: 0.741±0.023MJuptersol radius 0.723±0.026RJup for 1573 white dwarfs with 12000K≤T≤17000K; no radius measurement is possible for 26 white dwarfs with T<12000K. Using Monte Carlo simulations, we show that the observed temperatures and radii of SDSS white dwarfs are consistent with those predicted for compact objects made of hydrogen-dominated matter. The observed radius distribution is best reproduced with a single-component hydrogen model at 0.75<Γ≤0.95, with no additional components such as helium or metal contamination. There is no evidence for a radius increase beyond T≈17000K

"Bully" Star Attacked On Street, Cops Say

A Hollywood actor known for his role in the recent film "Bully" was assaulted Saturday afternoon in broad daylight in front of his home, police said.

Alexander Flores, 33, who was raised in South Los Angeles, told police that two men approached him while he was walking into his West Los Angeles home at about 4:45 p.m. and asked him for money, according to a Los Angeles Police Department report.

When Flores said he didn't have any money, one of the men - later identified as Cesar Altamirano, 39, of Los Angeles - knocked him to the ground, police said. The second man, who has not been identified, stood by and filmed the attack on his cell phone, according to police.

The first suspect started punching Flores in the face and head, investigators said. Altamirano, the second suspect, picked up a skateboard that was nearby and hit Flores in the head with it several times, police said.

The first suspect then took Flores' iPhone 6 and the second suspect punched Flores in the head a final time before they both fled in a black Toyota Corolla, police said.

*Acinetobacter baumannii* is an emerging multi-drug resistant Gram-negative pathogen that is responsible for a number of hospital acquired infections, including bacteremia, pneumonia, meningitis, and urinary tract infections[@b1][@b2][@b3][@b4]. Moreover, *A. baumannii* is also responsible for many treatment failures in intensive care units (ICUs), particularly in conditions with sustained low temperature, such as biofilms on heat exchanger surfaces in ventilators or catheters[@b5][@b6]. The intrinsic and acquired resistance to multiple antibiotics represents a serious threat to public health, and there is an urgent need to develop novel therapies to treat infections caused by *A. baumannii*[@b7][@b8]. It has been demonstrated that the cationic antimicrobial peptide plectasin possesses potent *in vitro* and *in vivo* activities against multi-drug resistant Gram-negative bacteria, including *A. baumannii*[@b9][@b10][@b11][@b12][@b13][@b14].

In recent months, Donald Trump has stepped up his efforts to woo the public by posting tweets and making speeches that seem to be appeals to nationalist sentiment. In the process, he has managed to unite almost all of the political spectrum against him, with those on the political Left and Right both labeling him a fascist.

This has given rise to the ridiculous claim that he is somehow leading a fascist takeover of the United Kingdom (or even that he is a fascist himself). This claim stems from a combination of assumptions. First, the claim is based on the premise that the British people actually elected a fascist to be their head of state. In fact, however, he was elected by a minority of Conservative Party members, not the electorate at large, and was already an established member of the party before the actual election took place.

Second, the claim is based on the assumption that the British national character is such that they would never vote for a right-wing conservative in a democracy. However, the truth is the exact opposite. The United Kingdom has a long tradition of conservative politics, and many people look to the Conservative Party as the party of traditional values. made with real Cheddar cheese

Ingredients

1 Pound Brick Cheese, cubed

1 Tablespoon Fresh Garlic, smashed

1 Tablespoon Fresh Parsley, chopped

1/4 Cup Olive Oil

1/2 Cup Dry white Wines

1/4 Teaspoon Salt

1/4 Teaspoon Freshly ground Black Pepper

Directions

Cube the cheese. In a gallon size ziplock bag, mix the cheese, garlic, parsley, oil, wine, salt and pepper. Seal the bag and massage the cheese to thoroughly mix the ingredients. Allow the cheese to rest at room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour. Chilling the cheese will slow down the cooking process. Serve the cheese with crackers or sliced bread.


Note: This recipe is easily doubled and freezes well.


Cheese Platter

"The Legend of Tarzan" is a 1958 film adapted from the book by Edgar Rice Burroughs. A sequel, "Tarzan and the Amazons", was released in 1954. Both films were directed by William Hayes and produced by Harry Saltzman and Jack Haley from a screenplay by John Gorham, based on the novel "Tarzan and the Jewels of Opar" by Edgar Rice Burroughs.

This is the first film version of the Tarzan character created by Burroughs. Jane Porter (played by Jessica Lange), who was engaged to Burroughs' hero, Tarzan, leaves her native England for the Congo and becomes his companion. When the British government declines Burroughs' request to create a Tarzan film due to the onset of the Second World War, he sells the story rights to 20th Century Fox, who produce the film. John Gilbert, who starred in one of the earliest Tarzan films, "Tarzan the Ape Man" (1918), reprises his role as Tarzan.

Story of The Day– “New Yorkers: This Is a Test”

In the latest episode of “New Yorkers,” hosts Andrew Cuomo and John Heil offer Brooklyn residents instructions for what to do in the event of a nuclear attack.

“If there is a nuclear attack, or a very serious danger of a nuclear attack, this is what you should do,” Cuomo says at the top of the episode. “Get outside. Do not shelter inside, a basement, a car, a building with a basement. If you are in a building that’s likely to fall, move to a window.”

Heil, meanwhile, notes that if a “large amount of radiation has been released, you could be exposed.” To protect yourself from radioactive fallout, he adds, “You need to get as far away as possible from the source of the radiation as quickly as possible.”

“New Yorkers” has, at times, struggled to strike the right balance between serious reportage and humor, but it largely pulled off this tense episode. The show did devote a bit of its runtime to a couple of “Weekend Update”-esque skits, however, in which Cuomo played a number of political candidates with names

Lifeway Foods , the operator of Church's and 365 Whole Food Market branded stores, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection, citing a challenging environment for the traditional supermarkets.

The company announced plans to close stores and expects to file a court-supervised restructuring by Jan. 31, 2022. In a statement, Lifeway said it targeted $125 million in assets through the bankruptcy process. "As one of the country's largest retailers of natural and organic foods, Lifeway is seeking court protection as it strives to restructure its business, operational costs and balance capital deployment with sales growth," the company said.

As part of its plan, Lifeway is looking to sell some of its real estate and lease back certain stores. The company said it would not close any of its existing markets. "We are hopeful that our stores will either be retained and returned to our lessors or sold to strategic retailers that respect our brand and mission and will continue to serve our customers and communities," the company said in a statement.

The company was founded as Living Word Christian Center in 1973 before changing its name to Lifeway in 1995 and entering the retail business in 2001. It operates 1,632 stores as of March.

Storytelling in virtual reality (vr) can be a powerful way to connect with audiences, immerse them in a story, and transport them to different worlds. This week, we are excited to share the release of Black Box VR, a first-person horror narrative experience coming to Oculus Quest this spring. Black Box VR will be available to play at SXSW, and is currently in development for other VR headsets like the Oculus Rift at the same location. Check out the teaser below and stay tuned for more information coming later this month at SXSW.

Black Box VR is a first-person horror narrative experience set in a decommissioned coal mine in Montana. A group of friends goes deep into the mine to explore and film a mysterious phenomenon occurring in an underground labyrinth. As the group explores, they begin to experience strange creatures, becoming separated, and eventually faced with a horrible truth. The experience is being developed using Unreal Engine and will run on Oculus Quest at 120 fps. For more information, check out the Black Box VR website.

VOICE ONE: I think of the ocean as my second home and it feels like I’m leaving something inevitable at home when I travel. But here in Fiji, you can be standing on a gorgeous white sand beach and the ocean could be minutes away. It’s so close I can almost taste the minerals, though I’ve never been able to confirm the rumor that you can taste where you’ve been in the sea!

My favorite way to see the ocean is with a boat ride. I’m not a great swimmer, and with the strong current in the water it’s really hard to make it to shore without a life jacket. But a boat ride is always fun and easy - you can just jump in!

I used to love snorkeling, but my mask kept fogging up and I got seaweed in my goggles. After a day on the boat, I was happy to just relax and enjoy the beauty of Fiji - all without having to put on a special suit or do anything active.

The thing I enjoyed most about our boat ride was when we went past a beautiful coral reef. We were so lucky to see many different kinds of fish

middle ages

A long time ago in a galaxy far, far away, a fearless group of travelers decided to brave a dangerous road in an effort to reach their destination. Unfortunately, their journey was riddled with peril as they were hunted by hostile creatures and plagued with various difficulties.

One traveler in particular, a mysterious young man by the name of William Shakespeare, faced incredible hardship and adversity during his lifetime, but he emerged victorious from this arduous journey and authored some of the most iconic works of English literature.

This course will provide you with a comprehensive survey of William Shakespeare’s life. We will explore how the events of his life influenced his works and how his works have shaped the way people view his life. By the end of this journey, you will have a far deeper appreciation for Shakespeare and all that he has contributed to culture.


What you'll need:

Reading

Listening

Writing

Workshops

Workshops will give you the opportunity to engage with me on topics related to William Shakespeare’s works. Here’s what you can expect from each workshop:

Workshop 1: Shakespeare’s Life Is Not What It Seems (50 minutes)

"Pump It!" is the twenty-seventh episode of the third season of the animated comedy series Family Guy and the sixty-first episode of the series overall. It aired on Fox in the United States on March 29, 2006. It features Peter trying to get Meg to sleep by taking her back in time to a more innocent period in history. When she gets there, she falls in love with her own image in the mirror and doesn't want to come back to the present.

This episode was written by Andrew Goldberg and was directed by Bobs Gannaway. It was announced as one of the nominees for the Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Animated Program in 2006. However, it lost to The Simpsons, the year's big winner.

Peter tries to get Meg to sleep by taking her back in time to a more innocent period in history. When she gets there, she falls in love with her own image in the mirror and doesn't want to come back to the present.

The title is a takeoff on the song "Pump,dump,dump,dump,dump" from the musical Grease.

public health risks associated with electronic cigarettes.

So, that's one more reason you should probably think long and hard before quitting smoking with electronic cigarettes. In addition to the many physical health concerns — like cardiovascular disease and cancer — there are more than a few public health risks associated with e-cigarettes.

Vaping Injuries

One of the more surprising public health risks of vaping is the number of injuries associated with e-cigarette use. According to the FDA, “In the last few years, the FDA has seen an increasing number of reports of lung injury and/or failure associated with e-cigarette use.” The agency even notes that “many of these reports indicate that the use of e-cigarette products was preceded by smoking traditional cigarettes in a “waking nightmare” that caused symptoms like shortness of breath, chest pain, and vomiting.” In other words, using electronic cigarettes seemed to lead to traditional smoking, with many users seemingly unaware that they were getting sick from using the same cigarettes that were harming their lungs.

made with love from my home in San Diego, California.

Menu

Tag Archives: oatmeal raisin cookies

I love making cookies, but let’s be real, sometimes it’s nice to have a sweet baked good that is less fussy and requires no special ingredients or equipment. These oatmeal raisin cookies accomplish just that. I’ve been making them for as long as I can remember and they somehow managed to change with me as I gained/lost weight, got married, and had my children. They are simple, they are delicious, and most importantly, they make every woman in the family think that you are a knight in shining armor when you offer to make these for the family.

Ingredients:

1 1/4 cups all-purpose flour

1 teaspoon baking soda

1/2 teaspoon salt

1/4 cup canola oil

1 cup packed brown sugar

1/2 cup granulated sugar

1 cup chunky-style peanut butter (do NOT use natural-style peanut butter)

1/2 cup milk

2 teaspoons vanilla extract

2 cups old-fashioned oats

2 cups raisins

"Bully" director Lee Hirsch has revealed that a sequel to his heart-warming comedy is in the works.

The movie, titled "Bully 2: Days of Renewal", will follow a boy who befriends a new kid and solve a mystery surrounding a bullying incident from his past, according to Yahoo Movies UK.

The news follows "Bully" writer and director Hirsch's appearance on CBS's Saturday Morning talk show "Sasquatch!", when he revealed the project's development.

"We're writing [the sequel] right now and we're trying to figure out what our release strategy is," Hirsch told host Daniel Kukurudz. "But it is with you, it's like your movie but with another kid."

"Bully 2" first came to light when it was announced at the 2018 Toronto International Film Festival, at which Hirsch appeared on the same stage for a special screening.

The first film, based on a true story, follows Rafe, who befriends Theodore, a new kid who moves to his suburban town, and takes him on a journey of redemption that culminates in a fight against school violence.

The original German article about a "German Shepherd" that attacked a woman in Seattle and broke her arm stated the dog's breed incorrectly. The Seattle Times has since corrected the article to state the dog was actually a Rottweiler. Rottweilers and German Shepherds are both breeds of Dogs, but are notoriously different breeds.


The actual breed of the dog attacked the woman in the Seattle neighborhood of Ravenna is Rottweiler.

private health insurance coverage by state

Private health insurance coverage varies significantly between the states of Australia. This guide provides an overview of the state-by-state breakdown of private hospital cover.

Note: this guide applies to private health insurance that is individually purchased and covers hospitalization, as distinct from a hospital or medical cover provided as part of a large employer group policy.

Please also note that, in NSW, Private Health Insurance Premium Tax (PHIP tax) applies to the entire cost of private hospital cover. This tax was introduced in NSW in 2012, and is charged on a per-taxable-event basis. In terms of the Private Health Insurance Act 2011, the PHIP Tax is not imposed on insurance cover for specified medical events (such as major medical procedures or pre-existing medical conditions) and is not imposed on claims for public hospital treatment.

NSW

Private hospital cover is generally only available for treatment in public hospitals in New South Wales.

Victoria

Private hospital cover remains predominantly restricted to treatment in public hospitals in Victoria. Exceptions include private hospitals in major urban areas such as Geelong, Ballarat and Melbourne, which have been able to secure additional contract revenue to cater for higher patient loads.

Anatid herpesvirus 1, also called the perch virus, is the most intelligent bird virus according to scientists at the University of Cambridge.

The recently discovered Anatid herpesvirus 1 was found to respond to stimuli in the environment in a sophisticated way, that would be similar to that of a vertebrate animal.

The discovery of the new virus was made when an examination of common carp tissues in the US revealed that it had contracted the virus.

The discovery of the new virus was made when an examination of common carp tissues in the US revealed that it had contracted the virus (file photo)

The virus, which is usually deadly for other animals, only killed the common carp.

Although the origins of the Anatid herpesvirus 1 are unknown, it is thought to have come from the natural waters of Northern Europe.

Professor Simon Armitage, from the University's Department of Animal Welfare, said: 'It may be too early to conclude that the Anatid herpesvirus 1 has a high degree of cognitive complexity, but some of its characteristics suggest this is a plausible hypothesis.

"Cinque" Centenario: A history of Milan through the centuries

The story of Milan - from the time of the Roman Empire, through the Middle Ages, the Renaissance and the early modern period and up to the present - can be summarized in the term “Cinque” (five). Starting from this root word, this blog aims to describe all the important events and turning points that have shaped the history of Milan.

Cinque Centenario: A moment of change

From the 5th of June 2013 to the 5th of June 2023

From a recent past, from the perspective of 2023, the history of Milan can be summarized as “Cinque”: five centuries have passed since the city was first mentioned in texts, since the construction of the medieval walls was completed and since the first mayor was appointed.

But in 2013, it was still possible to feel the echoes of those five centuries: new areas of the city were experiencing rapid urban expansion, and the process of gentrification was starting in those areas that were already well established. The urban transformations that were taking place were the result of decisions made at local and national levels, which had been translated in legislation and in the establishment of regulatory frameworks.

" Viva Lasers" was the motto of a series of public laser lighting shows that took place in the United States during the 1980s and 1990s. The shows were produced by National Laser Display Control Program, a private, non-profit organization. Lasers in the shows were operated by colleges, high schools, and other not-for-profit organizations. The laser shows typically featured competitions such as "power levels", in which teams would attempt to hit a moving flag with a laser-pointer-like device. 

The name, "Viva Lasers", was first coined at Christmas in 1987 at the Madison, Connecticut tree lighting when Robert Kraft, then Governor of Connecticut, turned on the first laser light show in the United States. The laser show was produced by the East Haven High School, East Haven, Connecticut. 

Many celebrities visited the sites of the laser light shows throughout the country and helped publicize the phenomena. The laser show in East Haven was visited by celebrities such as Meredith Vieira, Richard Belzer, and Joe Pesci. 

In 1988 a Viva Lasers tour hit 21 cities in the United States. The tour was made up of 15 lasers from high schools and six lasers from colleges.

It was released on 12 June 2018 as the second single from Harris's ninth studio album, Funk Waved Riddim (2018). The song was written by Harris, Smith and Jarrad Rogers, and produced by Harris and Rogers.

The song received acclaim from music critics, who praised Smith's vocals. "Holding On" debuted at number 87 on the UK Singles Chart, becoming Harris's highest-charting single in the country since 2008. It also peaked at number two on the Scotland Singles Chart, becoming Smith's highest peaking single in the country. Internationally, the song became Harris's biggest hit in Australia and his highest-charting single in New Zealand since 2008, while it reached number 19 on the German Singles Chart.

An accompanying music video for "Holding On", directed by Colin Tilley, was released on 12 June 2018. The visual shows Harris and Smith performing the song in a white room, with scenes of them singing interspersed with close-up shots of them. Harris performed "Holding On" at the 2018 Much Music Video Awards

"Cursed are those who trust in themselves, and not in God; for true faith shall lay hold upon everything, even upon death" St. Isaac of Syria

Prayer and Death

Prayer before dying is a practice which has been followed by many great people. It can help make us take our mortality more seriously, prepare us for what is to come, and give us a sense of peace in anticipation of what is to come.

The last words of great figures such as Plato, Aristotle and Sir Winston Churchill were prayers. So too was the last conscious thought of the scientist Michael Faraday who formulated the law of induction while praying in the garden of his residence at the Royal Institution.

“Notwithstanding the brevity of life, let us enjoy it whilst we may, and not repine at the removal of a burden which we know to be useful,” Faraday said. “To die is to go home.”

The 727-100 is used by Amelia Air for corporate flights, as well as for private flights, including passenger flights for the flight attendants and owners of the aircraft.

Tyler Cowen, economic historian, is famous for his "Things Fall Apart" series of books, which unpack complex social and economic phenomena with an historical narrative. In the books, he lays out a theory of how societies deteriorate in the long run, usually caused by some sort of "ism" or a "ism-ism" (i.e. Communism, Fascism, Capitalism, etc).

I'm not sure if "Things Fall Apart" has much to say about economics anymore, but I think his general point is still sound: human groups fail, and it's usually for the same reasons. A theory of history is not an ideology; it is a description of how ideology plays out in the real world.

Cowen is a smart guy with a lot of data driven insights.

"Titanic" was a grand success. It was a technical marvel, the largest, most expensive movie ever made. It was a thrilling adventure story, about two people who fall in love across classes and then must fight to survive the iceberg of class prejudice. And it was a heart-pounding musical event, with an amazing score by famed bandleader Ralphsky. Audiences of the day loved it — and the movie continues to give new generations of moviegoers thrilling adventure and romance on the big screen.

But not every critic loved "Titanic." British journalist C.M. Shattuck panned it in The New York Times, calling it "disquieting" and comparing it to Moby Dick and Frankenstein. And The New York Observer's Frank Smyth was also unimpressed, calling the film a "giant hunk of junk" and asking movie-goers to "get an HBO double-feature and save some money."

caravaggio

The works of Italian painter Michelangelo Merisi da Caravaggio (b .1597 - d .1671) continue to draw enormous interest from contemporary art collectors. Caravaggio, who was active from 1606 until his death in 1671, is sometimes grouped in with the Mannerists, due to the naturalism of his images and the dramatic lighting prevalent in many of his pieces. However, he is more properly regarded as one of the Roman Catholic Church's first real artists, painting what he saw during his travels through Rome's streets, in a style that was both realistic and dramatic. — Clarke Thumann

The answer - at least according to the book of genesis - is that man was intended to be nomadic, and had to settle down in one place in order to have children who could become civilized, building cities and all that jazz. There is no better evidence of the falsity of this claim than the fact that every civilization that existed before the Agricultural Revolution (around 10,000 years ago) was nomadic, and every single one of them had children and passed their genetic material on to the next generation without any need for building cities or civilizations.

And there you have it. The bible is the work of man, written down as a defense against the claim that primitive people weren't ready for "the land of milk and honey", and yet there is not one shred of scientific evidence to support the ridiculous claim that god needed to restrict the human race to only a "certain amount of days in the year" in order

"My sister and I were born on the same calendar day (December 4) but we took different paths in life. She married and had five sons. I married a schoolteacher and we had one son. Although we lived only a few kilometers from each other, we often felt like worlds apart. When I gave birth to our son, I was filled with a deep sense of happiness. My sister also had a son but she didn't share this happiness. She said that she felt like something was missing in her life and, after a brief period of visitation with her son, she gave him up for adoption. I was disappointed but, later on, I realized that her decision may have been driven by her own feelings of unhappiness. We may never know the truth but I hope she is happy, as I am with my five sons."

— Tadahiro, a 67-year-old Tokyo man, on the joys of having five healthy sons

Bash aliases are very useful, but they aren’t available in a non-interactive shell like a login shell. That’s why most shell scripting tutorials recommend setting up an ~/.bash_profile or ~/.bash_login to load aliases at login. But these files get sourced only when a shell is parsed, which may not be late enough for some aliases.

It turns out that one can create a new directory in your home directory, say .bash_aliases , and load aliases from there.

mkdir -p ~/.bash_aliases

Add the lines you want to alias in .bash_aliases/<aliases> . E.g.:

export HOT_KEY="Ctrl+t"

That’s it! At next login, your new aliases will be available to the shell.

Now you can use “alias” to load your aliases.

source ~/.bash_aliases

You can also use . ~/.bash_aliases ~/.profile and ~/.bash_profile if you want to share the same aliases across multiple

Aquamania – Sea World Orlando

SeaWorld is an aquatic theme park located in Orlando, Florida. It is home to a variety of marine animals and amphibians that live in coastal and ocean habitats around the world. This park is absolutely packed with rides, shows, and adventures for the entire family. We went for our first time a couple weeks ago, and I’m still kicking myself for not going with cousins as a kid. It was SO much fun. We were able to do the dolphin show, which was incredible. I’m still not sure how they do it, but they brought a few dolphins out on stage and they were able to perform tricks (like doing a high-quality back flip). The dolphin experience was definitely our favorite part of SeaWorld.

After the dolphin show, we toured the park. Everywhere you turned there was something fun to do! First, we saw some penguins. They were pretty funny, actually. They didn’t seem to care much for our tour guide.

Then, we headed over to the park’s killer whale habitat. I loved this area of the park. There

Our little family of four is growing this week with the birth of our sweet boy, Asher. We are absolutely amazed by this precious child and even more surprised by the speed with which our family is growing! We are over the moon with excitement and can’t wait to show you all of the sweet little things he / they will do.

Asher and his big sister, Claire, at 9 months

Asher was a little shy of his first Christmas but he quickly warmed up and had a great time opening his presents! (That’s his big sister, Claire, with the pacifier in her mouth!)

We love this sweet little picture of our girls hugging. They are nearly 18 months apart and are growing quite the little personalities! Claire is always telling Asher what to do, “it” and “put” and “go bye-bye”, and Asher is the proud big sister showing Claire all of the cool stuff he does. " The truth will set you free " is an over-used quotation in western culture, but it is undoubtedly true in this instance. After months of denials, the US government conceded that it had detained and tortured a number of people in secret prisons around the world.

Detainees were subjected to practices that were clearly antithetical to the America I know: brutal beatings; confined to small boxes for hours on end; stripped naked and made to hand over everything from gold dental fillings to blood samples.

At one facility in Poland, prisoners were held in underground cells the size of parking meters. Sound crazy? Well the world's most powerful nation, elected by a majority of voters, did exactly these things in the name of fighting terror.

This week's Senate Intelligence Committee report is damning. It reveals that the CIA's secret prisons operated outside US law with no effective oversight and minimal concern for whether detainees were "enemy combatants" or not.

The use of torture was "a near-absolute barrier" to obtaining intelligence, the report states, and it "played a significant role in impeding the broader fight against al-Qaeda."

The CIA was able to thwart terrorist plots because of the information it extracted

It came up unexpectedly and I was caught off guard. We’d spent the entire weekend apart, her in North Carolina with my parents and I back in Texas with my brother and his family. On our end, she was fine. We talked multiple times a day and had multiple video chats throughout the day. I spoke with her on the phone multiple times as well and she seemed fine, so I moved on with my life.

She texted me a few hours later with a picture of her with her mom. The text was mostly a mirror image of our weekend conversation - the typical “what did you do this weekend,” “me: nothing, you: same, here’s a picture” type of text; but the picture was of her and her mom with a serious, pensive look on their faces. She was squinting a bit, as if she was trying to see something in the background or was deep in thought. For the longest time, I thought they were looking at a mountain, but when I asked her about it she said they were actually looking at an old church. She was captioned the following:

“I found it, this place makes me feel at home.”

made from recycled plastic water bottles , and the first project of its kind from a university , the brain in a bottle opens the door to a whole new field of research and medicine. researchers hope the success of the brain in a bottle will spawn similar efforts with other organs. dan richman has the story. the first brain in a bottle is a milestone for a team of malibu entrepreneurs who say they want to do for the brain what peeing in a cup has done for peeing in a cup. the brain in a bottle project was the brainchild of dean poole, a former software engineer who runs malibu brainstem, along with partner dan radke and their team of scientists and engineers from across the country. the idea hit poole when he woke up covered in spit and burgundy liquid after a night of drinking. we must've hit a home run with this idea, he recalls thinking. why couldn't we do this for science and medicine? the team's first challenge was locating a dead rat that could brain-wash the bottle. soon after, poole says they hit upon the idea of a biodegradable polymer -- which they could use to make the brain-in-a-bottle container -- and settled on polyethylene glycol

made with a very smooth and creamy texture, and a mild, pleasant, earthy hop flavor. This one is best enjoyed on a sunny day with a tall glass of ice and ocean breeze.

The body and mouthfeel are thick and creamy, with a moderate carbonation, and a nice bitterness on the finish. This is a very quaffable IPA and would be great for a warm day.

Update (8/21/17): This brew was part of a mixed six pack I bought. While none of the others were exceptional, this one is very good. It has a clean, strong pine and grapefruit flavor. The malt profile is very biscuity with a nice balance of sweet and bitter.

I am surprised that most people like the bitterness of IPA so much that they continue to seek out more malt dominant examples. There is nothing wrong with a well bitter beer, but there are many wonderful examples of strong, flavorful, and well executed pale beers. I love this style and am glad to see it is still going strong.

deal with a different person, with a different identity. The "real" me is the one who works hard to live a moral life, treats others with respect, loves my family, believes in God, and has a clean criminal record. However, my life of crime is so well-known that nobody believes that I am this "real" me. So, I have to fake my death and start a new life. This is where the fun begins.


I get a new name, a new identity, and a new resume—all of which are carefully crafted so that I can achieve my dream of leading a quiet, moral life in anonymity. I move to a new city, get a new job, and rent a new house—complete with a new mortgage account, cell phone plan, and electricity account. I change all of my online credentials and social media presence. I take all of my professional and personal finances onto a new PayPal account and create a new, secret bank account. Any financial records from my previous life are shredded and disposed of. My old house is put up for sale.

Sustainable Development in Asia

In the 1970’s Asia accounted for 50 per cent of global carbon dioxide emissions. Today it is projected that Asia’s emissions will account for nearly 70 per cent of global greenhouse gas emissions by 2050. In 2016, Asia’s carbon dioxide emissions were 7.6 gigatonnes, an increase of 5.5 per cent over 2011 levels.

The region’s coal-based power sector accounts for the majority of these emissions; and though its contribution is projected to decline in the coming decades, it will not disappear overnight. This is largely due to the region’s growth in demand for electricity and the challenges associated with decarbonising this sector. A more robust focus on renewable energy sources is thus required.

Despite the rapid decline in prices for solar photovoltaic energy in recent years, investments in renewable energy have remained subdued in many parts of the region due to policy uncertainty, land rights issues and insufficient grid connectivity. Additionally, falling battery prices now make storage solutions more competitive. In line with the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development and its sustainable energy goal, the International Finance Corporation (IFC), a division of the World Bank Group, has identified climate-friendly investment opportunities in the renewable

deal with a problem. When faced with something they don't like, humans will often attempt to ignore it, pretend it isn't there or make up their own version of the problem (i.e. an imaginary friend). This behavior is called displacement.

 3: Displacement in VR A group of people are exposed to a stressful experience in VR. Through the headset, a person in the group's proximity is replaced with a anthropomorphic cat. When the cat appears, the people in the virtual environment behave as if the cat were actually there. They talk to it, pet it and engage inplayful interactions. When asked about their experience in the VR headset, the participants report negative feelings such as fear and anger. According to the results of this study, the cat in the virtual environment is an effective way of displacing negative feelings in VR.

rest of the meal. You should start looking for a new job right after lunch. You don't want to ruin your metabolism just for food. 3. Don't look at your phone

There's a reason why your mom told you to leave the room when there's a phone call. It's because you won't want to go to dinner if you see a new message. It's the same with your phone. Don't look at it for half an hour after lunch.

4. Go for a walk

It doesn't matter how short it is. Go for a quick stroll around the office. You'll burn some calories and will feel less hungry after that.

5. Drink two liters of water

You probably already know how important water is for your health. But did you know that you need to consume at least 2 liters of water per day to stay healthy? Monthly Archives: April 2016

We are all born with a set of natural strengths and weaknesses. As parents, we naturally gravitate toward the strengths and try to develop the weaknesses. In our house, we have a daughter who is good at maths and a son who is creative. We spend a lot of time encouraging and supporting the daughter’s love of maths and the son’s affinity for art. While the approach we’ve outlined has worked well for our family, what if your child has a strength in sports and a weakness in maths? Or a vice versa? As parents, we want our children to be happy, healthy and balanced, don’t we? Unfortunately, the secret to achieving balance in our children is not through supporting their natural strengths and trying to develop their weaknesses, but about cultivating both their natural strengths and their weaknesses. Research shows that cultivating children’s strengths is one of the most effective things we can do to help them achieve balance in their lives. Let’s explore why cultivating children’s strengths is so effective.

made with organic baby spinach, garlic, tomatoes, and mushrooms in a light balsamic vinaigrette. Serve it with a side of quinoa or farro, or add some quinoa or farro to the salad for extra heft!


This mushroom, garlic, and spinach salad is a great side dish or light main meal. It's easy to make, quick to assemble, and made with ingredients you probably already have in your fridge or freezer.

Baby Spinach, Mushrooms, and Garlic Salad

Author: Liz Weimers

Prep time: 10 mins

Total prepare time: 10 mins

Estimated serves: 10

Ingredients

1 lb. baby spinach

3 cloves of garlic, cloves peeled

1 lb. mushrooms (any flavor), sliced

½ cup balsamic vinegar

½ cup extra virgin olive oil

sea salt and pepper

Instructions

In a large bowl, add the spinach, mushrooms, peeled garlic cloves, and drizzle the vinegar and oil over the mixture. Add sea salt and pepper to taste, and gently toss.

Serve and enjoy!

made exclusively for Well Living, this easy breakfast is a complete meal in itself. It’s full of protein and antioxidants and will keep you satisfied all morning. It also only has four ingredients, which is ideal if you’re in a rush in the morning.


Ingredients

1-2 eggs

1/4 cup of chicken or vegetable broth

1/4 cup of grits

Salt and pepper to taste


Directions

Heat a saucepan on medium heat, misting with olive oil. Crack an egg into the pan and pour in 1/4 cup of broth. Allow the mixture to simmer for three to four minutes or until the egg whites become fluffy. Use a spatula to fold the eggs over so that the yolk stays intact. Remove the pan from the heat and sprinkle in grits and salt and pepper. Return the pan to the heat for a minute until the grits are warm. Spoon into a bowl and eat with a spoon.

Grits serve as a great protein-rich breakfast or snack, as they’re high in B vitamins and iron. When choosing grits, look for products that contain only three ingredients: grits, water, and salt.

Frank Hines

Franklin Delano Hines (November 7, 1888 – November 7, 1942) was an American race car driver.

Early life 
Hines was born on November 7, 1888 in Dayton, Dayton, Missouri to parents William Henry Hines and Elizabeth Jane Gandy. His father William Henry Hines was employed as a printer for the Dayton Advocate newspaper. As a young man, Hines worked as a farmer.

Racing career 
Indy car racing was in its infancy in the 1920s and 1930s, with few organizations developing the talent for open-wheel auto racing. Hines got his start in racing as an engineer or mechanic with the Mason Motorsports team. 

Hines entered the first Indianapolis 500 held in 1911 driving for Ralph DePalma on loan from the B.F. Goodrich Company. He drove a specially built Ruckstellers hydro-dome designed by Mason teammate Harry Miller in 1911 and 1912. In 1912 he finished tenth, his best career result.

The B-24 Liberator was first introduced in May 1943. The USAAF wanted a long-range strategic bomber, capable of penetrating the most sophisticated air defense networks and effectively perform long-range photo-reconnaissance and bombing missions.

The B-24 was initially deployed against Germany and to escort American bombers attacking targets in Germany and Europe. As American aircraft became more and more engaged in the European Theater of Operations (ETO), new versions of the B-24 were developed to handle the additional fighter protection duties.

A total of 8,795 B-24s were manufactured between 1936 and 1945; all served with USAAF except for 73 B-24Ks and 62 B-24Ms which were built for the Royal Canadian Air Force and Royal Australian Air Force, respectively.

The B-24 was one of the most produced American heavy bomber and played an important role in winning the Second World War. With its long range and heavy load, it was instrumental in both strategic and air support operations in all the major campaigns in Europe, North Africa and the Pacific.

It’s not difficult to imagine a future in which virtual reality becomes an indispensable medium for immersive journalism, interactive storytelling, real-time collaboration, and other forms of remote communication. In that future, the 360-degree video we currently see on social media might be packaged with software that lets users turn, look, or zoom as if they were actually present for the events being captured. Real-time translation software could enable people to communicate with each other as if they were face to face. And enhanced eye-tracking and voice-recording technology could allow people to watch and listen to participants in 3-D Virtual Reality and provide live captions in their own languages. As these technologies become more widely available and affordable, it’s easy to imagine a time when Virtual Reality could help us engage more deeply with history, culture, and society — and even allow us to step into the minds and lives of the extraordinary individuals with whom we share this world.

make pytest run fail fast if you import a module that does not exist

Details

Description

py.test's import and discovery mechanism makes it easy to accidentally import third party code that does not exist in your test environment. Since import errors are handled by Py.Test it can easily lead to broken test runs and poor monitoring.

I propose that py.test fail on import errors, make this an enhancement request since this could seriously degrade testing experience.

It is possible to detect unimportable exceptions via the importlib.Errors class. Most Python packages that are not part of the standard library should not be importing at runtime, so this should be easy to implement in a future.py.test.

This enhancement request is about improving the testing experience by detecting poorly written tests and fail fast. There are existing techniques to detect test failures (e.g. import failures). A failed test run isn't useful if you don't know about it.

I recommend that pytest run fail fast when import errors are detected. This will prevent broken runs and improve the UAT and monitoring experience.

Vote

Please vote on this enhancement request.

function strsub(s, start, len)

 static s, patt

 if len < 1 then len = s:len(start) end

 local res

 if s:sub(start, len) ~= patt then return "" else res = s:sub(start) return res:sub((len - (s:find(start, 1, '^') - start))) + 1 end

end

 Article summary - "Oxidative stress is one of the major cause of neuronal death and impairment after ischemia. Our research goal is to study novel compounds that is able to reduce oxidative stress and promote neuronal survival and recovery after cerebral ischemia. In this study we investigated the neuroprotective effects of a new compound 2-phenyl-1,3-benzoxazole-4-ethanol (CBR-22) in a rat model of ischemic stroke. The compound reduced the neuronal cell death by attenuating the oxidative stress through reactivating of antioxidant enzymes. mid-14c. (implied inME "woman" is from Old English wuwoman "human female"), from prefixed derivative of Indo-European base *wo-man (source also of Greek and Latin). Extended sense of "female person" is from 1590s; meaning "female occupant of a social position now typically filled by a woman" is from 1834; that of "female human" is from 1889. The noun is first recorded 1590s. Related: Womanism.

Late 14c. (in reference to the medieval "woman's movement"), from woman (v.1). Sense of "woman-only society or meeting place" is from 1650s. Woman to woman transmission (of information, advice, etc.) is from 2000s.

hospital. You probably won’t be ready to take on the day-to-day responsibilities of managing a business, and you might even find yourself in way over your head. But even if you don’t see your small biz taking over your life, it’s still essential that you treat it as if it could at any moment.

advertisement

Why? Because small biz owners are uniquely vulnerable to economic cycles, unlike large enterprises which have the money and scale to ride out most shocks.

It’s for that reason that entrepreneurial-minded folks like David Cancel and Dave Collum are so vocal about the need to protect your small business from shocks that could easily wipe you out–like a natural disaster or a pandemic.

What about the shocks we can manage? Many small businesses face unpredictable expenses and customer orders. But if you can at least partially forecast these costs, your business can at least partially adapt–you buy less inventory, cut order quantities, or shift work hours to reduce the impact.

But what if you can’t?

Enter the insurance company.

Most small business owners don’t have a professional CFO on staff. And if they do, that professional might be

"The Legend of Tarzan" is a loose retelling of the 1912 novel "Tarzan of the Apes" by Edgar Rice Burroughs. It was released by Warner Bros. in 1942, and is the sequel to the first film in the "Tarzan" series, "Tarzan the Ape Man" (1918). The Legend of Tarzan sees Tarzan searching for his lost family in the Congo, following a series of mysterious clues left behind by his late father, Sir George. This film also introduces Parker, a grown-up Tarzan son grown from a tree.

The Legend of Tarzan had a troubled production and was beset by controversy. Its leading actress, Maureen O'Hara, was given very little screen time and was not happy with her reduced role. As a result, she walked out on the production. For this reason, O'Hara was not invited to the premiere, and she did not receive top billing until fifteen years later when she was cast as an avenging angel in MGM's "Million Dollar Man" (1956).

The Legend of Tarzan was not a financial success, and the studio quickly put together another version of the Tarzan origin story, called

Story: The Storytelling Treehouse

This is a super fun sensory exploration that promotes imagination and the development of story telling skills! The Activity

Start off by finding a tree and laying the branches out in a circle, this will be their ‘stage’. You can use sticks to represent actors, or you can use play dough, that way the kids can dress up and act out their stories!

Once you have built your ‘stage’, ask the children to tell you who they think the actors are. They can tell you a story! If you would like, give them a prompt to start them off such as ‘I see a lion, a man and a fox’. This will give them a starting point and will help them to organise the story telling.

If everyone has a different idea of who is performing, ask them to point to themselves to tell their version of the story.

You could also get the children to come up with an idea for a story and then act it out with someone else. For example the first person could act as a lion, the second person could be a man and the third could be a fox.

Give the children time to talk and then ask who the first actor was, the lion!

rest of my family have been in B&NES for a lot longer then I have, so I don’t “get it” but am so happy to be living here! The friends and support network made moving here easy and fun!

I also love serving in our local Episcopal church, it’s so enjoyable to see students & church members connect & grow in faith! High school & church are both very demanding, but so fulfilling!

My favorite spot in B&NES is the “Lil’ Lemon” - we call it that because it’s a cute little house by the train station that is always decorated with welcoming flowers! It’s really a great place to pick up breakfast cookies, coffee, or other goodies whenever we need a little pick-me-up!

The terms civil union and legally married are sometimes used interchangeably, but this article uses the term civil union to refer only to an arrangement between two state actors, and so is not legally binding like a marriage. The term legally married is often used to refer to an arrangement between two people, which is similar to a traditional marriage but is also similar to a civil partnership and so not legally binding like a marriage.


A civil union is regulated in many countries by laws identical to or similar to the law that regulates marriage. The laws and regulations that define the status and nature of a marriage may vary between countries, and may be established by national, provincial or local governments, or by religious institutions. Typically, a civil union consists of identical marriage rights to those of a married person, but without the legal restrictions on divorce, separation

The amount of power that solar photovoltaic (PV) systems generate is based on a number of factors, including the solar modules' manufacturing efficiency, the properties of the solar modules, and the local conditions that the modules are installed in. Even when accounting for all these factors, it can be cost effective to invest in solar power, with grid parity often being achieved in many local markets well below the national average cost of electricity. Despite these cost savings, solar tends to generate more electricity during peak demand periods, which can lead utilities to discount the extra electricity generated by solar photovoltaic systems. This extra electricity can lead to grid overload and high electricity prices at other times, which can reduce the financial return on solar photovoltaic systems and slow the adoption of this energy source. Therefore, while solar photovoltaic systems can provide a reliable source of electricity in many locations, the variability in pricing and the interaction with the traditional electricity grid can make the financial return on these investments less than linear, which can reduce the overall investment in solar power.

Residential solar photovoltaic systems can consist of a solar panel, an inverter, and a meter. The solar panel is an assembly of solar photovoltaic

Lifestyle Changes To Keep Bones Healthy

1. Ensure You Are Getting Enough Intake Of Calcium

Calcium helps your body produce and maintain strong bones. It also helps the body absorb other vitamin D for strong bones as well. Foods high in calcium include dairy products, beans and peas, kale, salmon, and fortified foods like almond milk and orange juice. It’s recommended that you get more than 1000 mg of calcium a day.

2. Strength Train Twice A Week

Weight-bearing exercises help keep bones strong. Examples of strength-training exercises include walking, jogging, running, lunges, and step-ups. As you become stronger, you are making your bones more dense, which helps reduce the chance of osteoporosis, or brittle bone disease, later in life.

3. Don’t Smoke

Nearly every cigarette smoked reduces the amount of calcium your body can absorb.

4. Drink Plenty Of Fluids

Drinking enough water and other fluids also helps your body take in more calcium.

5. Use Moderate Force When Exercising

On Wednesday, President Trump held a ceremony to sign laws rolling back parts of the Dodd-Frank financial reform act. The event was held at the White House, and was live-streamed on social media. "By reducing unnecessary regulations, we will make it easier for Americans to create jobs, raise income, and develop real wealth," said Trump. The actions Trump signed into law will allow banks to make larger speculative deals, and also make it easier to borrow money from the federal government. The signing was cheered enthusiastically by financial sector employees, who packed the West Wing and hung President Trump photos throughout the offices. "This is a great day for America," said Gary Cohn, who was the Trump administration's first White House economic advisor. "We're cutting unnecessary regulation that limited our ability to make our financial system safer and more innovative. Now, banks can focus on finding novel ways to help working families protect and grow their wealth, instead of filling out endless paperwork and complying with arcane regulations."

However, the celebrations were bittersweet for the rest of America. Many in the public sphere saw the event as a capitulation to Wall Street, and a disturbing sign of Trump's willingness to make excuses for failure and criminality

name: CI tests

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - master
      - release/*
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 7 * * 0'

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - uses: nordegrim/scoverage-action@master
        with:
          coverage: 100

      - id: cache-s3
        uses: actions/cache@v1
         with:
          path: .cache/scoverage
          key: coverage-scoverage-${{github.event.committer.name}}-${{github.event.commit.id}}.txt
          restore-keys: |
            coverage-scoverage-

      - name: Install deps
        uses: actions/setup-go@v1
        with:
          go-version: ^[8.0]

      - name: Run tests
        uses: nordegrim/scoverage-action@master
        with:
          scoverage: github-pages

that awkward moment when you realize most people spell your name wrong and you're left feeling like an idiot because you went out of your way to put a unique spin on your name. also known as the name error pandemic.


yes, even i call it that.


i've seen a lot of different responses on this over the years. some people go to great lengths to hide their crazy spelling of their name. others embrace it and put a big, proud stamp on it (which boston robinson most definitely did). but many, many people go through life feeling like an outsider because they have an unusual name. and unfortunately, the name you were given at birth isn't nearly as unique as you thought it was until at least the 3rd or 4th grade.

but it doesn't have to be this way. there are a number of ways you can make yourself feel like you're not the only one with an unusual name.

1. don't worry about it or even comment on it. let people think what they think. you reap what you sew.

2. embrace it and make something of it. choose a nickname that people can easily spell and remember. call

Lifestreaming – Life on the Internet

What is Lifestreaming?

In the early days of social media, you followed the lives of friends and family via text messages or social networking updates. Then you watched events such as music festivals or political protests unfold on social media as these networks built profiles on your interests and interactions.

With lifestreams, users collect and publish information about their everyday experiences as soon as it happens – whether that’s a photo of their breakfast, a rant about politics or an ode to an animal. As soon as you sign up for a lifestreaming service, you become the curator of your own content experience.

Lifestreams are all about context. They’re about sharing exactly what you’re feeling at any given moment. They’re about being engaged, knowing and being connected.

Lifestreams have several names, including: “the moment”, “first-person narrative”, “360 Video”. Some describe the style as: time-lapse, stop-motion, low-tech TV; others, more futuristic, talk about the possibilities of using 360-degree camera technology.

middle and long-term care insurance — is a specialized insurance to protect families from economic harm due to the reduced income of a family member who needs long-term, continuous health care, which is out of pocket. Unlike hospital and major medical insurance, there’s no copay, no annual limit, and you’re not reimbursed unless your policy pays out. With long-term care, you are responsible for a guaranteed minimum and then your insurance company may still be on the line for additional, potentially large costs. Long-term care can include things like nursing home care, assisted living, adult daycare, residential care, adult basic education, home care, and medical equipment. The average annual cost of long-term care can range from $30,000 to $150,000 depending on the facility. Please see the links below for more comprehensive educational materials.

"If anyone had told me 10 years ago that I would be working in the coal mining industry, I would have said they were mad," said Chris West, a former colliery doctor for UK Coal. But then as now, the jobs were gone, and people were struggling to find work. "At the time I didn't recognise that I was working in a dying industry," he said. "But now, looking back, I can see the decline coming." Coal mining jobs have largely disappeared in the UK, with the last large scale mines closing in 2017 and 2018. But the decline of coal has been driven by broader changes in the power sector, which has increasingly decarbonised over the last few decades. Power generation has switched to gas, and to renewable energy in large part. But the shift away from coal has also been spurred by public opinion, which has become more concerned about climate change.

"The decline of coal is undoubtedly related to the broader issue of climate change, but also to public opinion," said Dr Adam Marshall, Director of the British Infrastructure Group (BIG). "If you combine a change in investor sentiment with rising concerns about health and climate change, it's a combination that's difficult to overcome," he said. Over

deal with China over its trade and technology policies.


The National Health Commission of China announced on Thursday that the country has released new guidance to improve the diagnosis and treatment of COVID-19, the novel coronavirus that has sickened millions around the world and caused more than 600,000 deaths.

The news was published on the website of the Chinese government, which has been accused of censoring information about the outbreak.

The guidance, according to a translation by the World Health Organization, calls for giving all suspected cases of COVID-19 “high priority” for testing, that health workers use “all available technologies” to screen patients, and that hospitals ensure there are enough medical supplies and testing equipment. The guidance also suggests “increasing health awareness education” and training for medical workers to avoid infection.

“These measures will hopefully improve the detection rate and treatment effect in China,” it reads.


China has accounted for more than 80 per cent of all COVID-19 cases and more than 50 per cent of all deaths. The World Health Organization considers it one of the most under-reported pandemics in history, in part because Chinese hospitals were not reporting all positive cases.

Sikeston Plating Company

The Sikeston Plating Company was an American industrial history museum and tourist attraction located in Sikeston, Missouri, United States, on the north bank of the Mississippi River across from Portageville, Missouri.

Incorporated in 1906, the plant began production in 1907 but closed during the nationwide steel strike of 1911–1913. The plant was reopened after the strike but closed permanently in 1917 after flood damage from the St. Louis Bayou. The Sikeston Plating Company was the focal point of the museum from 1917 until 1952, when the site was redeveloped as an armory. The property was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1977, and was individually listed on the State Register of Historic Places in 1979. The building reopened as the Sikeston Museum in 1983 and, following major renovations, today features a full-sized working diorama, a children's activity area, a gift shop, and an 18-room inn.

The plant was deemed notable for its role in the community, for its architecture, and for its location on the Mississippi River. It was one of the few steel plants on the river. The museum was opened by the Sikeston

validates email address conforming to a certain pattern

This module validates that the given email address conforms to a certain pattern with the following requirements:
- The email address must start with a letter.
- After the initial letter, only letters, digits and @ symbol are allowed.
- The special character '@' may only appear once.

Note that leading and trailing spaces are allowed.


Example:

good-email.com
hello@valid.email
hello@foo.bar
a@foo.bar
f00f.ipv4
foo@bar.com


Bad:

bad-email.com
foo bar@
foo bar
a.foo@bar.com
hello45@


Good:

good-email.com
hello@valid.email
hello@foo.bar
a@foo.bar
f00f.ipv4
foo@bar.com


Best:

best-email.com
foo@bar.com
hello@valid.email
hello@foo.bar
a@foo.bar
f00f.ipv4

Frank O'Hara (soccer)

Frank O'Hara was an American soccer player who played in the USL A-League.

Player

Youth
O'Hara grew up in Michigan and attended  Michigan State University. During his four years with the Spartans, he lost almost 70 games, the third highest total in school history. He played for the Michigan Bucks in the USL Premier Development League in 2004.

In 2004, O'Hara signed with the Harrisburg City Islanders of the USL A-League. Frank Lampard, who led the West Ham United club to an eighth-place finish and a cup-final appearance in his debut season as manager, has signed a new one-year contract at the Club.
