At a campaign rally in Arizona on Tuesday night, Trump announced that he was not considering a pardon for Arpaio.

"I don't want to cause any controversy," he began. "But I did want to let you know I am considering a pardon for Sheriff Arpaio."

There was confusion in the crowd, who cheered, "Pardon him! Pardon him!" At one point, one person in the crowd can be heard shouting, "Tell him to pardon the border!"

I cover the White House and I know how the announcement was received in the oval office. A spokesperson for the president said everyone in the room laughed at the comment.

"The president made a joke, the room laughed and then they talked about other topics," the spokesperson added.

The Denver Board of Education opened the 2017-18 school year with an $8.4 million budget, an 0.7 percent increase over last year’s budget, according to Jonna Spilker, the district’s director of budget and finance.
 Budget highlights include:
 Administration — A 2.9 percent increase over last year, with a salary increase of 3.8 percent. This includes pay hikes for the superintendent, assistant superintendents, and most staff within the central administration.
 Operations and Support — A 1.2 percent increase over last year, including additional funding for security and transportation.
 Facilities — Experience and energy costs have been reduced by reducing the number of buildings and consolidating offices. Staffing was reduced in non-instructional areas. Overall, infrastructure costs were increased by $300,000 to accommodate for improvements and repairs.

Finances 

The Denver Public Schools spend $17,865 per student, above the national average of $17,033 per student. The average class size in Denver Public Schools is 19.2 students, above the national average of 15.9 students.

 rich get richer and the poor get poorer eh?'

 'I agree, it's a sorry state of affairs, but that's how it is.' 
 But is it really how it is?  I think not, for there are many examples of very wealthy people who have come from very small beginnings, and in fact some of the most famous examples come from East London, namely EastEnders and The Queen's Royal Guard. 
 The famous EastLondon entrepreneur, Ronoga Sodhi, who was born in a council flat in Manor House, is a good example of someone from humble beginnings who went on to create success. 
 As a young boy growing up in Manor House I remember a man called Ronoga Sodhi, who came from a very modest background and lived in a council flat. 
 Ronoga worked at a sweet store for several years,  eventually buying the business from the owner.  He then went on to open a string of popular restaurants called Kismet.

text:
Are film trailers spoiling movies?
In April 2012, UK movie theater owners voted on digital screens to show trailers before movies started, and the vote passed with 84% in favor. Many cinema operators have complied with this legislation, although individual films may have alternative promotional videos.

More broadly, studies have shown that viewers have tended to appreciate the idea of previews more than they actually dislike the previews. A study commissioned by MoviePass found that 94% of respondents were either " satisfied " or " very satisfied" with previews. Previews may also help studios by increasing awareness of movies and the probability that a movie will be pirated.  This latter effect may be mitigated by using data from piracy monitoring services to identify and exclude viewers coming from pirated sources.

See also
 Trailers for movies, usually commercials for upcoming films shown before a movie is shown in a motion picture theater
 Previews, television commercials designed to promote a specific television program
 Posterization, a computer image editing technique used to transform a picture into a series of small images called "posters"

Quartz is one of the most common and popular minerals on earth, and it's basically a combination of silica sand and rock, which is mainly black in colour. Due to its widespread use and accessibility, Quartz has been dubbed the "most common rock" for good reason. The most common crystals are the Spinel and Garnet varieties.
 Sand, Stone and Sea - these three subjects are very closely related, and are amongst the most researched on Earth. The composition of Earth's crust can be determined by studying these three subjects.

Structure and composition

Quartz has the general composition of SiO2,  its molecules composed of four oxygen atoms and five hydrogen atoms. Its specific crystalline structure is described by the arrangement and orientation of these molecules. This structure consists of a basic framework of corner-sharing SiO4/ ions, which forms an almost perfect four fold rotation axis. The framework is completed by two types of atoms, oxygen and hydrogen, which are slightly different in shape and size and occupy specific octahedral and tetrahedral sites. These differing sites combine to form a network of solid and liquid channels throughout the crystal. 

The structure of quartz varies along different directions in the crystal. The simplest structure is seen in the c-axes,

After the martyrdom of St. Boniface and the fall of Mayence, the see of Cologne was vacant for thirty-one years, during which it was administered by deacons called the "Abbot-Princes" (Abboten-Könige). Finally, in 738 Archbishop Ecbert of Mainz secured the confirmation of his see's possession of Cologne and its dependent provinces, and the "Abbot-Princes" were replaced by autonomous Archbishoprics of Cologne, despite several counterclaims: that of the Bishop of Arles, that of the Abbot-King of Metz, and even that of the Carolingian King, Theuderic.
 Cologne become a crown land, with the King of the Lombards acting as Frankish archchancellor and the Duke/Prince of the Alemanni acting as the Frankish king's deputy.

Middle Ages

The Ottonian Cologne became the model for many cities that profited from the commerce or took power during the Middle Ages, often calling themselves "New Cologne" (modern-französisches Köln). Notable examples are Würzburg, Bruges

It is possible to erase up to 4 HDD or SSD volumes at a time (although only 2 at a time using Windows CMD). To erase 4 volumes, just repeat this step for each additional volume you'd like to erase.
 BCH erase is available only for devices using NAND flash memory.
 SSD erase is only available in Win10 1809 and later versions.
 SSD TRIM is not supported in all SSD controllers, and when it's not supported this command is similar to SSD erase.


### Example 1: Erase one hard drive 
This example shows how to erase a single hard drive.

1.  From anadministrator command prompt, type the command `erase disk`.
2.  In the `SelectDisk` dialog box, type the name of the hard drive to be erased in `C:` drive and press the `OK` button.
3.  Confirm the action by typing `erase C:` in the `DeallocateDisk` dialog box and press `Delete`.

After the hard drive is erased, it is no longer accessible. You can't mount it or access its data.

Meaningful, reliable and valid mRNA expression analyses in small amounts of fresh frozen and FFPE tissue samples can be performed using high-throughput sequencing. RNA sequencing can be used to define molecular signatures that are associated with histopathological tumor categories such as tumor grade. Furthermore, it is possible to predict the survival of CRC patients on the basis of these molecular signatures.
 {#s1}
=

### High-throughput mRNA sequencing in clinical samples. {#s2}

RNA sequencing is a recently developed approach for whole genome or transcriptome analysis, which is based on the sequencing of individual mRNA molecules and detection of their corresponding nucleotide sequences (Drummond et al., [@B11]; Hatzinger et al., [@B16]). This method is powerful, cost-effective and high-throughput even for clinical sample analyses of small amounts of material (Drummond et al., [@B11]).

In this study, we used next-generation sequencing in order to identify genes that are associated with histopathological tumor categories and to predict the survival of CRC patients on the basis of these genes.

We made it through another Queensland summer – even though it was the wettest on record – and then spent a few months in Melbourne, where the hot, dry weather finally pushed the bugs to the south.
 move to Europe

I've been looking for work in Europe since I finished uni. When I was sixteen, I dreamt of running away and living in Europe. I didn't know exactly where Europe was, but I knew it was somewhere with sun, beaches and food. My parents found out about the plan and made me wait until I was older to leave. They didn't stop me, but made me sign some ridiculous contract that I couldn't skip classes or change my phone number or address without their permission. I was so angry about this that I told them I would run away anyway. I packed my bags and left before they could change their mind.

Three years later, I still didn't know where Europe was, but I had learnt to love my new school, my new friends and my new home. One day, my dad found my empty suitcase in the garage. I guess he knew I was gone when I didn't come home for three days. I never told him that I didn't mean it. I really did mean it when I

text:
Weekend fun isn’t just for humans. Dogs love weekends too, and they love to enjoy the great outdoors. Unfortunately, as much as they love the great outdoors, they often suffer from heat exhaustion or heat stroke. To help keep your dogs safe and comfortable, take these 5 steps.
 create a safe retreat – Whether it’s a shady tree, cold water pool, or cool basement, create a safe haven away from the sun.
 cool down – If they become overly warm, dogs can sufferheat exhaustion and heat stroke. Lower their temperature by getting them to drink lots of water and moving them to a shady retreat.
 stay hydrated – Dogs can’t speak, but they can move if they’re feeling stressed or uncomfortable. Make sure they have plenty of access to water.
 lay low – If they’re inactive and too hot, they will become uncomfortable and possibly agitated. Make sure they have plenty of shade and water, and keep them as cool as possible.
 seek heat relief – If they still seem uncomfortable after several hours, check with your local vet. Heat exhaustion and heat stroke can be serious, even deadly, conditions.

There are a million ways that you can enjoy a fun and relaxing weekend. But,
prompt_prefix: Weekend fun isn’t just for humans.

text: I grew up with stove top mac and cheese. My mom would make it almost every night when I was growing up. I liked eating the cheddar cheese sauce, instead of the elbow macaroni, and I don't think she ever made it that way for me! I would ask her for "cheese sauce" every night, and even now I can't eat a big bowl of mac and cheese without the cheese sauce! And it's not every day that you find a stove top mac and cheese that actually tastes good. Well I found it! I can't believe how easy it is (seriously, 15 mins! And you don't even need a cheese fondue pot!), and how delicious it is. You can't even taste the elbow macaroni, it's that good. And of course it's totally loaded with nutty, cheesy flavor from the macaroni, and nice and creamy from the cheese sauce. It's the perfect anytime mac, and would make a excellent holiday dish! My kids, husband and I all love it! And it's totally customizable too - you can use any kind of cheese you want; as much or as little as you want; and you can add as much or as little pasta you want!

text:
Do You Need Residential Roofing Contractor?
 a.  The first question that you should ask yourself, before hiring any roofing contractors, is whether or not you really need a roof replacement. Roof damage is generally not visible, and therefore, you may want to try to repair the damage before you spend on a full replacement. However, if you notice missing shingles or broken seals, you should contact a roofing contractor immediately. Roof replacements are very expensive, so you do not want to wait to have the damage fixed - you will probably end up paying a lot more than if you had spent the money up front to have the repair completed first.

What to look for in a residential roofing contractor?
 b.  In addition to asking if you really need a roofing replacement, you also want to make sure that you are working with a licensed, reputable contractor. The most efficient way to work with a roofing contractor is to have a detailed, written contract that clearly outlines the work to be completed, the payment arrangements and the warranty information for the completed work. Always ask to see proof of licenses and insurance before giving them access to your house.

A:

Yes. It's certainly advisable to try for pregnancy before cryopreservation and transfer. This isn't always possible, of course, but most clinics will allow you to freeze one day's worth of eggs (sometimes called a "blastocyst freeze"). Given two fresh attempts at ovulation induction, you may be able to conceive naturally in that timetable. Then you can transfer a blastocyst at your three month transfer.

The Arlington County Board plans to vote Saturday afternoon on an agreement reached by the County and the Department of Justice (DOJ) to reform the county’s mental health system.

Under terms of the agreement, the DOJ will withhold approval of two ongoing consent decree modifications if the county fails to enact sweeping changes to its mental health system.

The first modification, which covered the period from 2015 through 2018, was originally filed in July 2017. It recommended changes intended to reduce racial and social disparities in the mental health system.

According to a report released by the DOJ in April, progress was made in some areas yet significant disparities remained overall. The agreement was scheduled to be reviewed again in November and finalized in December.

But in a joint motion filed Friday, the two parties agreed to settle the matter instead.

“The County has taken significant steps to address the concerns raised, and as a result, the parties do not believe that the modified consent decree should be further modified,” the motion states. “In light of this change, the United States will not challenge the County’s decision to modify the original consent decree for the 2015-2018 period subject to the strict deadlines therein, unless a material change occurs.”

The Missouri DLD is the only department in the United States to test actual DMV-issued drivers for blood alcohol content, and to collect the test results as part of the routine driver’s license background check. The unit was formed in 2009 in the wake of Missouri’s devastating 2008–2009 wine country woes. The DLD conducted more than 1 million tests between 2009 and 2017, at a cost to consumers of $12 million. text:
New research from the University of Illinois in Chicago suggests that the more people exercise, the sleepier they tend to get.
 past studies on the effects of exercise on sleep have produced mixed results. Some research has shown that exercise increases total sleep time and deep-sleep percentage, while other studies have shown no effect on sleep.

In the new study, participants completed activity tracksers and sleep diaries. The researchers analyzed data from 50 participants, and found that on nights they exercised vigorously, they slept an average of 50 minutes more each night than on nights they spent doing no exercise. 

According to the authors, this is because exercise releases "sleep-inducing substances" such as endorphins and human growth hormone. This stimulates the release of melatonin, the hormone that helps induce sleep.

The study is weak in that it had a small sample size and had no control group. Moreover, one of the assumptions of the study is that vigorous physical exercise stimulates the release of sleep-inducing substances, which in turn stimulates the release of melatonin. However, vigorous physical exercise can also increase the release of other substances such as adrenaline and noradrenaline (also known as nr adrenaline or simply adrenaline), all of which can induce wakefulness

The Christmas Market is guaranteed to set the Christmas mood; traditional and modern stalls fill the market selling an abundance of handmade and traditional products, from paintings to ceramics, woodworks to textiles.
 o Christkindlesmarkt Ludwigshafen – Ludwigshafen
The Christmas Market in Ludwigshafen am Rhein is the oldest in Germany. Started in the 12th century, the Christkindlesmarkt is one of the largest and oldest markets in Europe. The traditional and popular market is situated in the historic center of the city. It is one of the most beautiful and romantic locations in Germany hosting around 300 stalls and hundreds of thousands of visitors every year. The Christmas Village is a great attraction for children and offers many activities and games. There are also a lot of very traditional souvenir and food stalls. In the background of the Old Town, the famous Ritterquelle fountain, which is fed from ice in winter, flows.

 Westphalia – Düsseldorf, Essen, Aplerbergen

Justice studies is an interdisciplinary area that blends topics from many other social sciences to provide a more complete and balanced approach to understanding the complexities of the criminal justice system. CJAs must be aware of concepts and theories, but they also must be able to apply multiple theories flexibly as the situation requires to effectively and efficiently analyze and interpret data. The CJA/PMP examination tests analysts’ and managers’ understanding of several theories, their history, and their applicability to today’s criminal justice and public management issues. The ability to apply theories flexibly is the most valuable skill analysts and managers can have.
 past years’ CJA/PMP examination questions demonstrated a lack of knowledge regarding the theories applicable to the criminal justice field. In particular, questions on the applicability of cultural theory were a recurring issue. This year, candidates were asked to explain how cultural theory could be applied to specific issues within the criminal justice field, such as reducing incarceration and the racial disparities in the criminal justice system. Cultural theory, which began being applied to criminal justice issues in the 1980s, does not account for the wide range of diversity found in society. Trying to find a way to say "thank you" made me wonder about other ways I could show my appreciation. The more I thought about it, the more I realized I was already doing many of the things I loved most. I could think of specific people in my life who have made a difference in my life and I wanted to show them my appreciation.

I decided to start a giftcard campaign. I'd been given giftcards for my birthday, Christmas and other notable occasions throughout my life. Rather than just using the giftcards to go toward a large purchase (like an appliance or other item I was planning to buy) I'd exchange them for something small but meaningful to the donor.

I started with the giftcards I had received for my birthday and Christmas, but eventually added in giftcards from friends, family and coworkers. Over a span of 11 months, I gave away over $120 in giftcards. The total sent away exceeded $160 because I gave one giftcard to a friend who was moving to a new city and didn't have any local spending money. The giftcard could be redeemed at a store in her new city.

I decided to keep track of the giftcards I swiped in this blog post.

text: Chronic Kidney Illness (CKD) and End-stage Renal Disease (ESRD) affect millions of Americans, imposing substantial burden in terms of human suffering, cost and healthcare utilization. One potential solution to reducing CKD and ESRD burden is reducing the incidence of hypertension by preventing chronic kidney damage in at risk populations. The long-term objective is to bring novel, affordable and accessible non-invasive early detection and screening tools to prevent CKD and ESRD through early intervention and target organ preservation. The project will focus on evaluating the efficacy of detecting early impairment of renal function using non-invasive protein-to-creatinine ratio (PCR) in the general population. SpecificAIM 1: Demonstrate that the urinary protein-to-creatinine ratio (PCR) is a valid, accurate, cost-effective and non-invasive measure of GFR in the general population. We will conduct a multi-center study to assess the association between estimated GFR (eGFR) based on the urinary PCR or serum creatinine and measured GFR (mGFR) obtained from inulin clearance in a large bi-national general population of adults. text:
In 1997 Ronald Phillips published their first annual catalogue attracting attention amongst UK skateboarders for their inventive promotional videos and distinctive clothing. Phillips’ use of humour and pop-culture references saw them become a pioneering name in the emerging UK skate scene and gain a dedicated following of "Phillip-heads".
 2012 saw the release of their first video part for the American rock band Green Day, appearing on the track "Hitchin' a Ride". The video received over 300,000 views in its first month alone, subsequently being featured in the annual top 100 lists of both VH1 and MTV. 
In 2014, Phillips collaborated with the British footwear company Sole Collector on a limited edition range, designed in conjunction with their in-house graphics team and featuring an adapted version of their "Spitfire" logo. The range was released in conjunction with a series of "guerilla" street art paintings around London, one of which was painted on the back of Phillips' shop front in West London.
A solo exhibition of Phillips' artwork, curated by fellow Green Day fan Danny Wynn, was subsequently held at the Fountain Studios in London in 2017.

text:
A probation violation can occur in a number of different ways, including (1) the offender being arrested by the probation department for violation of the probation agreement; (2) the probation officer submitting a written report to the court that the probationer has violated the terms of probation; or (3) the court finding that the probationer has violated the terms of his probation based on a hearing with the probationer and the probation officer present.

 the probation officer submits a written report to the court, the process for determining if a probation violation has occurred begins with the court receiving the report. The court will typically set a supervised hearing at which the probationer and the probation officer will appear. The hearing is usually conducted by a magistrate judge, a specially trained judge who conducts hearings and makes recommendations to the district court. The primary purpose of the hearing is for the magistrate judge to assess whether the probationer committed the alleged misconduct based on the probation officer's report and the probationer's admission, denials, or explanation. In some cases, the probation officer may testify and present other forms of evidence to support the allegations against the probationer. The magistrate judge will make a recommendation to the district court. If the probationer argues that he did not violate the terms of his probation

"Dozens of network-attached storage (NAS) devices from dozens of different manufacturers can all run OpenStack, and every OpenStack deployment is different," said Alan Moss, CTO of Cloud4Co, an OpenStack consultancy. "The devil is in the details, and each customer will have their own unique set of requirements that need to be evaluated carefully when selecting an architecture or hypervisor."
 vendor neutrality is a relative concept. While many large NAS vendors have embraced open source technologies like KVM, they still require that customers stick with their proprietary NFS and iSCSI SAN storage protocols.

Because each NFS/iSCSI gateway supports a single proprietary protocol, it's not yet possible for two NFS/iSCSI gateways to communicate with each other via an open networking standard. Vendors of open networking standards, such as Openstack's own Neutron, are exploring techniques to support multiple proprietary NFS/iSCSI gateway protocols over a single open networking standard.

University of Utah track and field athlete Lauren McCluskey has died, school officials announced Wednesday.
 age 20, McCluskey died Tuesday night due to an “incidental hanging” at her apartment in Salt Lake City, according to a statement from the University of Utah.

“We are heartbroken to share the news that Lauren McCluskey passed away last night due to an accidental hanging,” the school said. “We would like to offer our sincerest condolences to Lauren’s family and friends. The University of Utah is providing support for her friends and teammates as well as the team during this tragic time. The University has support available 24/7 for anybody wishing to access support during this time of great grief.”


McCluskey was a member of the Utah track and field team and lettered in cross country as well, school officials said. She is the third Ute athlete to die in the past two years, following former gymnast Nathan Walker in February of 2018 and baseball player Jake Diekman in June of 2017.

“Our thoughts are with Nathan and Jake’s loved ones, and all of the Utah athletics community during this extremely difficult time,” the university said.

Economy
The primary industry in Sheridan and nearby communities is agriculture. THE STORY: Bob was born in Fort Cobb, Oklahoma, and began performing at an early age. By his teens, he was playing guitar for Roy Buchanan and The Guess Who. In the early 70s, he teamed up with Jim Kale to form the band Storm. Kale was soon replaced by Dave Aucoeur. Storm became notorious for their hard partying and unpredictable behavior, which sometimes got them banned from concert venues. Their first album, Caught in the Act, went Platinum, and several of their songs were top 40 hits. In 1978, Bob, Jim and the rest of the band reunited, in one form or another, for the album, Live!
 also went Platinum. The live album was recorded at The Forum in Inglewood, California on June 15, 1978.

In the early 2000s, plans were made to release a 16-track version of Live! on CD, with more complete setlists and studio outtakes. However, these plans were scrapped when MCA, the album's original distributor, went out of business, forcing Rhino Records to acquire the rights from bankruptcy court. Rhino released a remastered version of Live! on CD in November 2016.

On November 2023, Microsoft released the Windows Subsystem for Linux version 2 (WSL 2), after beta testing the release for more than a year. 

Unlike WSL 1, which used a version of the WSL Microsoft launched in 2016, WSL 2 is based on the new Azure Marketplace version of the WSL, which was first released in 2022.

Pastel painting

Although some consider Pastel a medium suitable only for study purposes, it has also attained a high level of artistic recognition, especially in the last few decades. It is known that Picasso and Braque, among others, used Pastel to develop their artistic skills.

Pastel has an odourless, slightly chalky consistency and produces a velvety sheen on the paper. Its opacity allows to modulate the intensity of light. It is usually coloured with cacao, saffron or violet or primed with pumice or sandpaper to allow the addition of glaze or charcoal. The art of drawing with Pastel, called pastelism, has its own special technique and aesthetic.

 Mavrick (Marshrutka) - is a transportation system in Saint Petersburg, which serves as both a bus and a trolleybus. It is colloquially called marshrutka ('ma-rasrut'ka) in Russian. It is an ancient form of transport, existing in Novgorod, Pskov, and other cities since the Middle Ages. Initially, marshrutkas were horse-drawn caravans, but over time they have gradually become motorized. In Saint Petersburg, marshrutkas operate every five minutes on the central ring road around Saint Petersburg, and every ten minutes off the central ring road. Some marshrutkas operate as bus lines within the city, and these lines are occasionally served by trolleybuses. All of the above lines are operated by RATP Group.

The experiment was a failure, but we all learned a valuable lesson about the dangers of old plastic soda bottles.

text:
The Western Shoshone call themselves Newe, or Nane, and are one of the federally recognized tribes of Western Shoshone people. Their current reservation is in the Black Rock Desert region of northern Nevada, near the town of Ducktown, where they have a government town and tribal park."
 Art Bell, a radio host, promoted a Western Shoshone episode in 2005. The episode alleged that elements within the U.S. government and military had conspired with oil companies and other interest groups to create a "new world order" and enslave the Western Shoshone; it urged the tribe to take up arms against these enemies. Bell claimed that Art Bell garnered details of this plot from a "very reliable source within the tribe". The Bell episode was controversial and mocked extensively on  the internet; this led to Bell cancelling his monthly Shoshone program and later withdrawing the episode from syndication. The controversy also prompted Native American groups to condemn the New World Order theory and suggest the conspiracy theory was used to distract the Shoshone from their real enemies.

injury We use a combination of techniques for the treatment of soft-tissue injuries, including RICE (rest, ice, compression, and elevation). RICE is very effective in reducing inflammation and swelling. However, inflammation is not the only cause of pain and swelling; sometimes injuries can result in cuts or tears in the skin or underlying tissues, which may also experience inflammation. These types of injuries are called bruises or lacerations.
 Pain and inflammation can keep you from engaging in routine activities, like working, exercising, or enjoying pleasureable activities. 
 The more you rest, the more your body will heal itself. It is very important to follow the instructions given by your doctor or physical therapist.

Medical care
The treatment of soft-tissue injuries commonly follows the RICE principle, which stands for Rest, Ice, Compression, and Elevation. The rest period can last for as short as 8 hours or as long as 21 days, depending on the severity of the injury and the individual's recovery goals. It is important to realize that resting does not mean resting on a couch; it means resting from the activity that led to the injury, as well as engaging in other activities that do not cause the injury to worsen.

text: SRID is a tedious and time consuming assay to perform. In this study, we report a novel approach to perform a rapid screening of anti-trypanosomal activity of plant extracts in bloodstream forms of Trypanosoma brucei using a luciferase reporter strain. We evaluated 1413 plant extracts from a biodiversity-rich national herbarium as potential sources of new anti-trypanosomal compounds, and report seven extracts with a notable inhibition of T. brucei growth. We also report four extracts that decreased luciferase expression in an EC50-independent manner, which highlights the necessity to further assess the mode of action (MOA) of active extracts. This luciferase reporter strain could represent an alternative to the conventional, more time consuming, method based on determining parasite growth in the presence of extracts (static-adapted assay).

For infants the operation normally can be done at 18 months of age provided that the child is growing normally.

The key steps involve opening the chest and detaching the right and left handed parts of the heart. The abnormal ventricular septum is then removed and the valve leaflets strengthened. The right and left hands are then sutured back together. After the operation the lungs will be collapsed with an external device but normal breathing can gradually be resumed alone. The child will need a pacemaker for several years.

text:
Is an election recount a good idea?
The short answer is no. In the United States, we conduct elections with a high degree of trust that the process is not susceptible to fraudulent activity. We achieve this trust by using a high volume of easily reproduced, optically scanned paper ballots, with a rigorous chain of custody procedure to ensure that the ballots are not tampered with and can be reproduced accurately. The election night results are then the result of a comparison of these optically scanned paper ballots with the electronic files from the voting machines. When problems arise, we examine the results and the underlying systems to correct for errors.
In the event that fraud is suspected, a recount assumes that fraud occurred, re-scans the ballots with the same high degree of accuracy, and finds additional fraud. Unfortunately, all of the aforementioned procedures to achieve trust in American elections are undermined by the recount proposal.
First, the recount would require the machines to be rebooted, destroying the original results and requiring the creation of new ballots images. This means that the original optical ballot images are no longer available for verification, and the new ballot images must be compared without access to the original comparison. This is like finding a stolen painting with a copy, rather than with the original. Second, the chain

The location of the July 15, 2002 flare is marked by a yellow arrow. The yellow lines are the solar loops connecting regions of opposite magnetic polarity. The black regions are associated with negative magnetic polarity and the blue regions with positive. The large oval marks the location of the July 28, 2002 flare. The white line is the potential magnetic field. The red regions are regions of strong shear. The green oval marks the location of the C1 coronalloop viewed days later on July 31, 2002. This region appears sheared but does not develop into a coronal mass ejection. Credit: NASA
 created using data from the Solar Dynamics Observatory (SDO) and the National Solar Observatory.

Solar flares are powerful bursts of radiation, light, and heat from regions on the sun characterized by increased magnetic energy. The energy typically goes into heating the magnetically active regions at the core of solar flares. In addition, flares produce bursts of radio noise and accelerating particles. 

Solar flares occur when twisted magnetic field lines become too unstable to continue to exist. In some cases, loops of hot gas become so twisted that they break the magnetic force field that holds them close to the sun and loop out into space. When this extra energy is released it becomes visible as a solar flare

However, I’m not convinced that the solution to the problem of large associations can be found within the four walls of a building. We have to take a step back and examine associations and business in general from a different perspective.
 new perspective on associations
I believe the problem with associations today is that we have adopted the business model of the 20th century (product -> customer) rather than the 21st century (customer -> product). By product I don’t mean physical goods. I mean the offering of some intangible that you can buy. Your skill for skiing or playing piano, or your expertise in a specific topic area, or your access to a private plane, to name a few. The customer for this product is typically another person. When a person purchases my piano skill, or my skiing expertise or my access to a private plane, what they are really purchasing is the time I can spend with them. And the value I can provide them.

Local government

Gelligaer was created as a municipal borough on 1 April 1898, with an elected mayor and borough council. It was granted city status on 18 July 1907. Subsequent changes to the borough and the abolition of the municipal borough name came about with the passage of the Local Government Act 1974, as part of the new county of Gwent.

Gelligaer was made a district of the new county of Gwent, and a local government district of South Wales. The Gwent authority, which included neighbouring Neath Port Talbot county borough covered most of the existing Gwent district, but not the neighbouring county borough of Port Talbot. An uncontentious boundary change took place at the same time as that resulting in the abolition of Gwent County Council, when the neighbouring county borough of Port Talbot was brought into the Gwent area by virtue of the boundary change of 1 April 1974.

The Graduate Law degree is a graduate entry course in Ireland and UK law degrees that is intended as an advanced legal course of study. The course has an extensive written component, leading to a Graduate Diploma in Laws (GDL), and is complemented by a two- or three-day oral examination held towards the end of the fifth year of study. Upon successful completion of the Graduate Diploma, students are awarded a Graduate Diploma in Laws, though some institutions subsequently offer the opportunity to take a Master's degree in addition.
 Law Degree – Law is primarily a course of study for those interested in pursuing a career in law. The course typically lasts between three and five years, and comprises coursework and an assessed final examination.
 LLM (Master's degree) – The LLM is an interdisciplinary masters-level program that allows students to specialize in a particular field of law or in legal research. The LLM is offered jointly by two Irish universities, University College Dublin and the University of Liverpool.

In Australia and New Zealand, the legal program consists of four years of study, leading to a master's degree in law (MCL). In addition, some law schools in Australia offer an accelerated six-year course to a Doctor of Juridical Sciences

text:
On of the key elements for most cloud solutions and that is data security and consistency – at on-prem you have full control and can do whatever you want with the data, and that is not the case in the cloud. In the cloud, data is stored in multiple locations, multiple versions and multiple formats, all of that needs to be kept consistent for all the use cases – eDiscovery, data analysis, machine learning and so on. This consistent view of data requires additional controls and work by the stewards of the data to make sure that the data is available in the right formats and in a consistent state. Additionally when companies are looking to use or migrate to the cloud, IT teams need to ensure that all the on-premises workloads are fully vetted and validated to ensure that they will work in the cloud, a process is known as cloud migration. Without these two requirements, you cannot use cloud as a complete solution for data security and consistency.
 cloud governance and compliance – All the data in the cloud needs to adhere to the security and governance policies of the organisation that is responsible for the cloud deployment. This includes authentication and access control, data classification, retention schedules, etc. If you are still doing all this in your own data centers, you can

 Options:

    -hash           Hash file contents using MD5 or SHA-1.  This option
                    is supported only on Unix-like systems.
    --help          Show help options.

 Usage:
    fileutilize [options] --recursive [--exclude pattern] [-e enc]
                    -regex pattern   Generate hash value from file name
                    -regexOptions options
    -hash           Hash file contents using MD5 or SHA-1.  This option
                    is supported only on Unix-like systems.
    -exclude        Exclude files and directories, default is *
    --help          Show help options.

    --recursive     If set, operate on directories and their contents too.

 Examples:

text: Do your knowledge workers need a shared call queue? For many companies, the need to centralize communications around a specific topic or agenda has led to the introduction of call centers. The call centers exist in various forms: an 800 number with a virtual assistant, a central voicemail box that gets messages forwarded, or a virtual call room in Slack. While these solutions have saved companies the trouble of managing individual phone threads of individual knowledge workers, they still suffer from a central access point lockingknowledge workers out of conversations unless they are available. This means knowledge workers have to juggle multiple individual threads -- one for each topic they are assigned -- which is hard to organize and leads to knowledge workers frequently missing important updates due to their lack of organization. In short, call centers solved the problem of managing phone threads, but not knowledge worker availability: an even bigger problem.Now, let's consider the problem from another perspective: end-user experience. Today, when a knowledge worker (here, also referred to as an "end-user") enters a shared call center room, several things can be distressing for them:1. They have to constantly request more people's attention, slowing down their participation2. They might not know if there are any new updates to the conversation,
prompt_prefix: Do your knowledge workers need a shared call queue?

text: A simple framework for developing a concept beneficiation strategy is described below. First, a preliminary beneficiation plan can be devised, based on the mine type and grade of material to be treated, with the aim of recovering at least 65% of the mineral fraction. With the preliminary beneficiation plan in hand, one should review the operation and assess whether there are opportunities to improve recovery. For example, if the preliminary beneficiation plan includes winnowing, one should assess whether alternative techniques such as magnetic separation or flotation could be employed. It may also be possible to improve recoveries through changes to the beneficiation strategy such as increasing the particle size of the material to be treated, since smaller particles recover at higher rates. Once the improvements to the preliminary beneficiation plan are assessed, a revised beneficiation plan should be formulated. Ideally, one should compare the recovery results from the revised beneficiation plan with the recovery results from the preliminary beneficiation plan. The results of this comparison would indicate if further improvements to the beneficiation strategy are required. Summary: An introduction to historiography and the major historiographic methods.
 focus: on basic terms and concepts associated with historiography and the major methods of systematic inquiry;
 extent: basic terms and concepts are introduced; discussion of historiographic principles and methods is encouraged; application of historiographic principles and methods to specific issues is not required.

Themes

Thematic history 
Thematic history focuses on one major topic or theme, such as the theme of migration.  Although this type of historical analysis typically focuses on a single topic, it can also be pursued theoretically by examining the relationship between a series of related topics. Thematic history can be usefully employed in understanding a complex range of topics or a complex time period. An example of the use of thematic history is in the work of Fernand Braudel, who examines the link between land and sea power in 15th century Europe.  

By focusing on one topic or theme, thematic history can help overcome the limitations of more general approaches which might examine a huge chronological or geographic scope but which might fail to encompass some of the important changes taking place within a particular time period or spatial area.  Thematic history thus offers great potential for historical discovery.

 L.A. County Sheriff Lee Baca has retired more than 50 staff members after an inspector general's report found serious misconduct in his personnel office. KPCC's Sharon McNary has more.


FULL TEXT (Article 1):
Today, I am announcing my retirement after more than 26 years of service to the County of Los Angeles.  This was a very difficult decision, but based on the findings of the Inspector General, I can no longer serve as Head Sheriff.  My resignation will become effective at the end of the month.

I want to thank the Board of Supervisors, the Chief Executive, the Law Enforcement Officers and Deputies for their unwavering support over the years.  Your commitment to duty, integrity and compassion has changed the lives of so many people for the better.

Finally, I would like to comment on two key findings of the report, which stated that there is no evidence of criminal activity or corruption.  In fact, two of the sheriff's captains were quoted in the report as saying that the sheriff had a 'culture of fear.'  I cannot refute that, nor can I defend that culture.

When I assumed office, I

We're a group of volunteers who have had the incredible experience of caring for a premature or sick baby in our neonatal NICU. For the baby, it could be the strongest pull of love you will ever witness. For us as parents, it could be our darkest time yet filled with many unexpected twists and turns.

helpBabiesNow was founded because we felt there needed to be more support for parents who have been through this experience. Our organization’s sole mission is to help other parents with newborn babies who have health challenges, learn from those with similar experiences, and find community and connection through online resources and offline events.

Our goal for helpBabiesNow is to continue to help parents, especially those who have a baby with less than perfect health or a disability, by providing them with the emotional, practical, and informational support needed to help their baby thrive.

In China, the prevalence of tooth decay among 12-35-month-old children was 53%, and the main types of decay were fissure sealant decay (15.5%), baby bottle decay (14.2%), and incipient decay (12.8%) \[[@B8]\]. The main reason for tooth decay is that the diet of Chinese children contains too much sugar. The early detection and treatment of tooth decay can effectively avoid the occurrence of serious consequences such as abscesses and tooth loss. However, most children do not have a regular dental examination before they are 3 years old, and the decay is then found at a late stage when it is more difficult to treat \[[@B8]\].
 Children's oral health has a significant influence on their overall health. Dental decay and poor oral health can cause systemic infections and affect the child's development and cognitive functions. It will also cause secondary infections in the body if the decay reaches the pulp tissue \[[@B9]-[@B11]\]. Long-term neglect of children's oral health can result in malocclusion, which may lead to speech problems and restricted facial growth. Signs and symptoms 
Primary hyperparathyroidism most often presents with the classic signs of hypercalcemia: bone malformation (e.g. pathologic fractures, renal stones), kidney stones, abdominal calcification, proximal muscle weakness, mental changes (e.g. irritability, confusion, depression). However, the symptoms are frequently vague and non-specific.

Associated disorders

Pathophysiology 
In primary hyperparathyroidism, a lesion in one of three cell types produces excess parathormone, which causes hypercalcemia:
 Parathyroid adenomas – produce parathormone primarily from inadequate synthesis by the parathyroid glands.
 Atypical adenomas – produce little or no parathormone, but may secrete epidermal growth factor, which leads to the rare tumorous form of the condition.

MARIKINA CITY – The Marikina City Jail, home to some of the city’s most notorious figures, has been renamed the Victor Wood Justice Center in a ceremony on Tuesday, Jan. 22.
 held at the Marikina City Hall.
The renaming of the jail, which was spearheaded by the Social Justice Group in partnership with the local chapter of the National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI), was attended by officials from the national government, and the local government of Marikina.
“Today, we not only rename the Marikina Jail, we also honor Victor Wood, a national hero, in the same spirit as how we are trying to address our collective issues of crime and incarceration, which the Jail was founded on, as a social issue,” said NAMI-Marikina chapter head Karla Abceda.
Wood, a New Jersey-native, was a decorated World War II veteran, and a member of the U.S. House of Representatives who fought for social justice reforms. He was exposed to the horrors of Nazi concentration camps, and was one of six U.S. Congressmen who voted against an appropriations bill to fund the construction of the notorious Nazi death camp at Auschwitz.

text:
The Telemetry Distribution Board is a very useful addition to the Alamofire framework. It provides a consistent way of sending and receiving data over an HTTPS connection and monitoring it using a REST API.
 monitoring APIs with Alamofire

- Enable Logging
- Subscribe to Data
- Delete Data
- Get URL Data

# Enabling Logging

The Telemetry Distribution Board provides a way to enable sending usage data to configured backend services. These backend services can be used for logging purposes only or reporting data to external parties.

First, instantiate a DataMonitor instance:

```swift
let telemetryMonitor = TelemetryDataMonitor(metricsConfiguration: metricsConfiguration)
```

This instance needs to be added to your Alamofire configuration:

```swift
Alamofire.Session.default.dataMonitor(with: telemetryMonitor)
```

This will submit all data received via requests to the configured backend service.

**Note**: Telemetry data can contain personally identifiable information (PII). Under the [EU GDPR](https:// GDPR.CO.UK/), Alamofire takes appropriate measures to ensure that PII is removed from requested data
prompt_prefix: The Telemetry Distribution Board is a very useful addition

The Duplicates by VIN report shows invoices which are considered duplicate by the system based on the following matching rules:
 match when:
    the merchant and customer are the same
    the transaction amount and reference number are the same
    the currency are the same
 when the merchant and customer are the same, the invoices could be the same if they are old or there could be differences between them for example the details of the goods and services exchanged.

So, how Duplicates is intended to be used really depends on the kind of business you are in and what are the typical kind of invoices that your business might produce.

A:

Invoice duplication is a very common occurrence in almost every business. The question then is how to identify duplicate invoices while minimizing false positives? This is where the uniqueness of each invoice comes in.
Using the customer ID as a key (the Primary Key) and the amount as a checksum (the secondary key) combined with the checksum validity interval, you can build a mathematical model that would enable you to identify potential duplicate invoices with a very high degree of certainty.

Before engaging in any intellectual property-related work, you should always research the status of protection for any relevant work you will use.  Failure to do so could result in your work being subject to infringement and the infringer may be contractually or otherwise liable for damages.
 this article is about copyright protection; for infringement of other intellectual property rights, see  infringement.

Intellectual property (IP) is the property right in an original idea or other expression of information or creative thinking. It can include inventions, words, artwork, sounds, and copyrights.  The purpose of IP rights is to promote the creativity and innovation that benefit the wider society and result in jobs and commercial activity.

You can register a patent (US) or a copyright ( worldwide) to establish rights to your invention or creative work. Registration permits the owner to enforce the rights using the courts and to prevent others from copying the work.  It can be done without cost or obligation and, for copyrights, registration provides protection for the creator as well as the public

 a) No Digging: to avoid the disruptions & cost of digging, we can install real Time Locating Pipe Marking Systems on-site. This process involves the delivery of radio-frequency signals via specialized pipelayer vehicles from an antenna located at the work site.The signals are reflected off of the pipes and received by a mobile data collection unit. The operator of the data collection unit can be accurately map the location of the pipes while avoiding any digging.

b) Sewer Line Repair: In some cases, excavation is still required to locate and repair the broken sewer lines (or lateral lines). With Exchange Server 2013, there are certain scenarios wherein you may need to apply incremental changes to a Mailbox database. One of those scenarios would be when there is a new Mailbox version applied to the Hub Transport server, and you need to update your clients to the latest version. This blog will walk through that process via the Deployment Wizard.
 step 1. First you'll need to enable the Legacy CDO interface. This can be done using the Set-Mailbox cmdlet with the legacy cdo interface parameter.

After enabling the Legacy CDO interface, you can deploy a new Mailbox update by using the Deployment Wizard. This will install the latest Mailbox version from the Exchange server, along with any updates that have been published to the Exchange Organization since the last time this server was updated.

To deploy a new Mailbox update using the Deployment Wizard

1.	Log into the Exchange Management Shell as an administrator.
2.	Run the New-ExchangeServerSoftwareUpdate cmdlet.
3.	Set the Exchange version and optionally the language the user interface is displayed in.
4.	Select Custom and specify a path to the .xml update file.
5.	Run the Update-ExchangeServer cmdlet

I found an interesting article here.
The article is based on a study by Dolly Chiu of UC Berkeley and Alex K. C. Lee of the University of Washington. They analyzed 12,000 people from around the world, representing 140 different countries, and asked them a set of questions that reveal an individual's cultural mindset.
Based on this, they were able to create a Cultural Mindsets Index (CMI).
They found that the following cultural mindsets were most important for success in a country:

 Independent self-construcion
 Achievement-oriented
 Responsible freedom

For example, independent self-construction refers to the extent to which people in a country value individuality and autonomy. A score of 100 represents a country with predominantly independent self-construcion, with lower scores representing cultural values more focused on tradition and obedience.
Independent self-construction was most important for success in... 
Chile, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Jamaica, Mexico, Panama, Paraguay, Puerto Rico, Trinidad and Tobago

Why Many Businesses Choose to Incorporate in Delaware
 Article from Forbes describing some of the main benefits of incorporating in Delaware for a growing business.  

Incorporating in Delaware provides many business benefits.  For example, according to Inc 500|5000, Inc. 500 rankings of the largest private companies in America, more than half of the state’s companies are located in Delaware.  Another plus is that the state has no income or franchise taxes.  Also, since incorporation filings and tax documents are public, it’s very easy to get visibility into a company’s performance.  

Another reason more and more companies are incorporating in Delaware is that it offers a “Single Corporate Residency” option, which streamlines the process of establishing a corporate presence in the United States.  A business can use this option to meet the needs of both resident and non-resident companies, making it a popular choice for cross-border businesses.

The health minister is on-record as stating that the government is looking at introducing dedicated rehabilitation clinics in all hospitals to ensure that patients with non-life-threatening illnesses are not routinely discharged but are instead guided through rehabilitation programs, which are critical for their overall recovery, and management of comorbidities. Such a model is already in place at several Western countries’ centres of excellence in rehabilitation medicine. This announcement is encouraging, given the current lack of such clinics and the significant readmissions that could potentially be prevented for non-life-threatening illness such as dental and eye infections.

We would like to see dedicated rehabilitation clinics implemented at a minimum for the following categories of patients: those with (i) neurological conditions, such as strokes and traumatic brain injuries; (ii) orthopaedic conditions, such as joint replacements, limb salvage after major injuries; (iii) cardiac conditions, such as myocardial infarctions, cardiac ablations and valve replacements; and (iv) respiratory conditions, such as lung transplants and certain cases of patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.  ## Local Files Support
We can now support loading andsaving of local files!  This is a **very advanced topic**, so make sure you have the prerequisite skills first.


1. Create a local directory named `ch9data` in your user profile folder `~/.config/nvim/` (or `path/to/nvim/profiles/<username>/.config/nvim/` for systemwide `nvim`).  Paste in the following contents:
    - **Sample file structure**
        ```
        .
        └── ch9data
        ```

    - **Sample `ch9data` directory**
        ```
        .
        ├── colors
        ├── doc
        ├── ft plugins
        ├── indent.vim
        ├── syntax
        ├── syntax/hiccup.snorris
        ├── plugins
        ├── test.txt
        ```

made globally competitive, the first major economic update in the 21 st.


President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday oversaw the first major economic update in the modern era that is also intended to make Russia globally competitive.

Speaking at a meeting with Prime Minister Dmitry Medvedev, first deputy president Vladimir Putin oversaw the release of the government's draft economic strategy for the next 10 years.

The aim of the strategy, according to a copy of the decree posted on the government's website, is to "safeguard the leading position of the Russian Federation in the sphere of military-technical means and services and ensure its sustainable economic development."

To achieve this, the government plans to focus on innovation-based development, attract foreign investments, promote production in Russia, and expand international exposure for Russian goods.

The new strategy is largely based on the concept developed by Russia's Security Council, which Moscow established in 2006 to guide the nation's security policy.

The Security Council has drafted strategies for development, military-technical, social, and economic spheres and has set a number of goals, including to make Russia a leading global player in high technologies by 2024.

mid 14’century and remains unfinished by end 17century due mainly for funding Reconsideration: the initial proposal for the building was prepared in 1362, when Leonardo had about 30 years old, and it was continued by different engineers during his lifetime but with little results; recent restorations completed in the 20th century have damaged the original architectural composition. In 2002 a study about the feasibility of a full restoration was begun by the  “Studio d’Arte del Leonardo”, in cooperation with the “Fondazione Leonardo” (an Italian foundation created by the Leonardo da Vinci National Academy of Sciences) and it was concluded that only partial restoration would be feasible, on condition that the original structure would be preserved. Restoration project was started in 2017, and is expected to be finished in 2023. Restoration project also made public possible the knowledge about the original materials and structures, which are made up by marble, brick and steel.

Significance and impact

public employee – namely a person working at a salelier as an agent for the government, rather than for an employer – has the same First Amendment rights as does anyone else. These rights do not evaporate simply because the person is paid with public funds.

That makes this case worth your attention. The decision is here.

Two issues are presented in the appeal from a ruling by a federal trial judge: (1) whether the public employee retains his or her free speech rights when speaking as an employee; and (2) if so, whether the speech at issue in this case was protected by the First Amendment.

For most of us, the answer is yes – public employees do retain their free speech rights while working.

But the answer is no when what is at issue is criticism of one's employer or of public policy issues applicable only to one's employer. When a public employee crosses that line, his or her First Amendment interests give way to the employer's interest in efficient operation of the workplace. In cases of that kind, the employer's interest prevails.

When public employees give public speeches, their expression may still run afoul of other rules, but the First Amendment is rarely a barrier. A public employee speaking to

"I’m never satisfied” is always uttered by human creatures for better or for worst. This is more evident on the people around you who are constantly comparing and seeking for the better, looking for the best and never quite happy with what they have. For them, the “I’m never satisfied” motto seems to make sense. A better way to look at the phrase may be “I’m never content”. Contentment is a state of mind. If you truly are content with what you have, nothing material could ever make you “never” content or satisfied. Being satisfied and being content are two different things altogether.

This is true for physical possessions as well as experiences. A vacation destination that you didn’t enjoy, a restaurant that didn’t live up to your high standards or even a friend who let you down may make you never satisfied. However, these instances do not make you content. You may have had the best trip to Hawaii but if your friend could not make it, you will still be never satisfied even with all its great beauty. Being “never content” seems to be a more accurate way of describing people who always need more, who are never satisfied and always in the pursuit of the ever better, the next best,

second root when nine none twiitter followers. In the first tweet after being retweeted, the user specified that the follower was the second root, giving the user a nice shoutout and a bit of fame.

However, the elation soon turned to panic, when the user checked his follower count, and noted that it had decreased by 2! I hope this be a lesson to all users to be careful about what they wish for, as it might just come true.

public relations agency in Paris in 1881, initially acting only the local network promoting local events or personalities. In 1898, after the merger of two other agencies, it became the Public Relations & Advertising Agency of Paris.

The agency provided services mostly to the trade, including exhibitions and commercial presentations. In 1900, it became a member of the Association des gens de la publicité and, in 1902, joined the International Institute of Public Opinion.

From 1912, its name was Publicity and Advertising Company of Paris or, simply, Pan-PAC. The agency supported the French army during World War I, through the creation of a fund for public donations. In 1920, it created the Société d'assurance pour les produits d'industrie françaises, renamed in 1924 as Assurance Maladie Paris.

After the World War II, Pan-PAC focused more on public relations and public advertising, developing its network in foreign countries. In 1965, it changed its name to Pan-PAC Public Relations Agency. In 1971, it was acquired by the Publicis agency and, since 1974, is part of Publicis Worldwide.[2]

make g() (fun { p x } else raise err ), for all types ` x ` and functors ` p ` and ` err `; where ` g ` is fresh, i.e. doesn't appear in ` p ` or ` err ` or in any of the variables they bound. In this way we can have a fresh ` g ` for each invocation of ` fun { p x } else raise err `. It is guaranteed that each fresh name is unique: names cannot be rebound across different closures.

make g() (fun p x => ...), for all types ` x ` and functors ` p `; where ` g ` is a fresh name different from ` p ` and ` x `. Like before, ` g ` is guaranteed to be unique.

loob (LOOV_) prefix "lotto/" = lottery+ lot; "loob" is the Korean word for lot. Lotto is a typical game of chance widely played in many countries across the world - particularly Europe and Latin America - and currently offered by most lotteries in Korea as well. Lotto is typically played with random drawings of numbers, often between 1 and 69, and players select one or more numbers to play. The odds of winning a prize are influenced by the number of players selecting a particular combination of numbers and the drawing prices of the lotto tickets are typically increased the more players select the same numbers. The most popular lotto game in Korea has 6 numbers with a prize pool of KRW 10 billion (~ USD 10 million) and is currently being advertised with the tagline "The Big Win" ().

Recent events have called into question the ethics of this game, and there is a growing trend towards abolishing the lotto and its prizes altogether, and instead using the money for improving the welfare of the people through various social welfare programs. Such was the case in the 2018 presidential elections in South Korea, where former candidate of the opposition Minjoo Party of Korea (MJP, now known as the Democratic Party),

Frank Rizner’S thoughts on Trump’S Executive Memorandum On “ExecutiveAmnesty”

Donald Trump’s new Executive Order entitled “Ensuring Public Safety in the Intermediate Security Period” is causing a lot of consternation and confusion. The media spin is that this is President Trump abandoning his campaign promise of deporting millions of illegal aliens. In fact this is the complete opposite. The actual order merely clarifies and expands an already in place 2012 program called “Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals” or DACA.

This 2012 Administration program allows for the deportation deferment of some 800,000 young persons of Mexican, Guatemalan, El Salvadorian, and Honduran ancestry who came to this country as young people, mostly without papers. While it allows them to work on a temporary basis, it does not allow them to receive public benefits, including government-subsidized college tuition. Trump is merely rescinding an overbroad, unconstitutional, administrative rule from previous Presidents, and leaving this program alone.

It’s true that this 2012 order did not specify that DACA was a deferral only of removal in the event of non-criminal behavior — rather than simply deportation in most cases. However

air traffic radio server is a plane type which allows to listen to a variety of radio channels as they are available in the aviation industry. This includes both civilian and military communication such as ACARS, ADF, AIS, RNAV, RNP, UNICOM, VSAT and many others.

Installing the air traffic radio server role on your FreeIPA server allows any user on the system to activate the ACARS dbc mode and listen to these radio channels. This can be done by simply issuing the following two commands in a FreeIPA session:

air ctra sk --server x.x.x.x --role aircraft\ traffic_radio_server role plane type

air ctra ipa sk --server x.x.x.x --role aircraft\ traffic_radio_server role plane type dbc mode ACARS

The German regional court of Munich ruled on Tuesday in favor of Ryanair in a dispute with the European commission, suspending two sanctions imposed on the Irish low-cost airline last March.

According to a press release sent by Ryanair, the European air navigation authority (EANNA) and the European aviation safety agency (EASA) "took the unprecedented step of applying to the German regional court of Munich for a preliminary injunction, trying to force Ryanair to immediately cease operating its flights."

Because this was an error, the command-line interpreter displays 'more options' as well as 'hello world!' to indicate that the command was successful, but did not complete with the expected results.

* xtensa llapi timer running 16 cycles. (2c latency).

 * xtensa llapi timer running 32 cycles. (4c latency).

 * LSUPI timer running 1 cycle. (1c latency).

 * The timers with lower latency are preferred for most applications.
However, LSUPI has very low overhead and guarantees a minimum
granularity, which may be desirable in some cases.


Xtensa LLA and LLAPI are tools that allow a programmer to implement
a timer in software on an Xtensa processor. LLAPI provides a
high-level interface to configure and run timers, while the LLVM
backend in LLA provides the implementation of the timers.

Xtensa LLA is available as open-source under the GNU GPL v2 and
comes withbuilt-in tooling to compile, link, and test timers. It
supports both the LLAPI and LL kernel interfaces and can be used to
develop and deploy timers in production systems. The LLAPI interface
is the recommended choice because it provides a higher level of
abstraction and easier understanding of the implementation, while
the LL interface is potentially more flexible and efficient

make tuxedofs - try (hd device dev name) format && reboot (device will be named tuxedofs once reboot completed)

example (hd cdrw0):

sudo bless -format /dev/cdrw0 tuxedofs

sudo reboot

The device will be named tuxedofs once reboot completed.

Note that the device name will look like this /dev/tuxedofs

To make it easier to type, you can add the name to your `~/.bashrc` or `~/.zshrc` to make it autocomplete when you type the first few letters.

source <(sudo bless -format - )

Note that you will need to be root to use this command, either use sudo or prefix the command with your users password.

Frank Kamenarac died 10th January 2003 with his fursuit still attached during his performance at the Zpoc Anime Con in Austin, Texas. The fursuiters were forced to leave the stage while waiting for a new PA system to be installed.

An autopsy later revealed that Frank had heart failure due to chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).


The fursuiters did manage to go on stage with another PA system and finished the con.


Frank was an original Megaloth.


He was a member of the fursuit group: Megaloth

He was best known for being in a few episodes of the anime TV series, Fushigi Yûgi.

Megaloth was a fursuit group made up of Frank Kamenarac, John Carpenter, Brian Schultz, and Matt "Bear" Baker. They are most famous for appearing in an episode of Fushigi Yûgi.


At the Zpoc Anime Con in Austin, Texas on 10th January 2003, Frank suffered a heart attack and died. He was 38 years old.


He is survived by his wife, four cats and two dogs.

We left the station early in the morning, and travelled as far as Luz by daylight, reaching our destination for the night about 9 p.m. We did not rest as we were eager to arrive before dark, for fear of travelling through a country inhabited by Tentmakers,* who are the most dangerous robbers in South America.

. Here we were obliged to remain all day next morning, as the station is at a great distance from the town, and nobody came to attend to the wants of passengers. We hired an ancient, dilapidated van, and made several necessary purchases; and then after resting an hour, again set out for the station, hoping to accomplish our journey that night. But our horses were so jaded with hard travelling, that though we made every effort, we were unable to accomplish more than ten miles a day.

“… to die of curiosity….”

Theodore Sturgeon

The old farmer scratched at the itchy soil with his trowel, the clap of his palm falling on the head of the trowel ringing in the crisp morning air.

After the harvest, he’d returned to the small cottage he’d been keeping an eye on since moving to this land decades earlier. The cottage was tucked away on the other side of the orchard; the only way you’d ever see it was if you happened upon the meandering dirt track that meandered through the orchard.

As he watched now, slowly a small grey door knob turned. The door opened a few inches, and through the crack a single eye studied him.

After a moment, the eye disappeared and a small, slim leg poked through the opening, followed by a furry grey body. The cat rubbed against the wooden door, leaving a grey streak on the edge of the door as it rubbed against it.

“Oh, there you are,” intoned the voice of the old man. Noticing the cat rubbing against the door, he reached into his pant pocket

Lifetimes & Settlenances #LifeAfterGen
=============================

Our grandparents and great grandparents lived shorter lives. The average lifespan was about 40 years back then. Nowadays, the average lifespan is about 80 years; women live on an average of 86 years and men live on an average of 81 years, even though they might look much younger. This difference is mainly due to the invention of modern medicine and medical technology. Thanks to antibiotics, we can live much longer. But even though we live longer, we don't live as long as our grandparents did. The average lifespan today is still considerably shorter than it was one hundred years ago.

Why is longevity increasing so dramatically? Is it possible to live as long as people used to live 100 years ago? In medicine, we can already cure or at least manage most illnesses. Diseases are now treatable, although there are sometimes limits in our ability to treat certain illnesses.

Even though it is becoming more and more feasible to live long lives, we don't live as long as we used to live 100 years ago. The maximum lifespan nowadays is about 115 years, and people are generally considered to have reached old age at around 90 years. However, we have not found the secret to longevity yet,

loffoo wrote:"What's really interesting - and a model everyone in Japan can take inspiration from - is their public transportation system. I've lived in many cities around the world where people hate getting around on public transportation, but it's never worked as well in every city. But in Japan - even though the trains and subways can get pretty crowded - the system is so efficient that everyone uses it." Read the rest of the story from our partners at the Asahi Newspaper.

It's so interesting to hear about how the Japanese are solving their transportation problems. I live in Singapore, and we've built the world's largest rapid transit network, but many people still find public transportation cumbersome and inconvenient. I think more people in Singapore would use public transportation if it were more reliable and convenient.

So I hope the governments of other underdeveloped Asian cities take a page out of the Japanese book and invest more in public transportation so their populations can enjoy the benefits of fast, frequent, and modern transportation systems as well.

second derivatives implied for two variable system and Hess(Second Differential Of Energy). I find many values with zero(not all) which make the Hessian matrix not positive defind. What could be the problem?

When your hessian is not positive definite, you need to find the local minimum by checking the value of hess[i,i]. If the value is 0, then there is no need to worry because its a degenerate case and the value of function at that point can be calculated by the values of function derivatives at the point.


You need to check the values of other elements of hessian if all of them are 0. If some of them are 0 and the rest are negative, then again there could be two possibilies. One is that the point is a relative minimum and second is that there is a bug in the implementation. In a nutshell I can't say for sure what the issue might be.


I hope this helps.

factual evidence which supported their testimony”). In any even., a state has wide ber; rights regarding the custody and care of its children. Here, the Supreme Court found that the constitutional right of M.O. and the petitioner to raise their child in a home free from drug use and abuse was overridden by the DHHS’ decision to remove the child from the home. This, the court found, “requires more than just a showing of theoretical safety risk, there must be evidence that the child was actually exposed to, affected by, or harmed by” the parents’ drug use. M.O. v. Cty of Denver, 907 P.2d 71, 74 (Colo. 1995) (en banc). The petitioners, M.O. and the minor child, had been using drugs outside of their home and in the presence of their children. The State, through the DHHS, demonstrated actual harm to the children by presenting evidence that: the parent’s conduct endangered the children’s health and welfare; the conduct exposed the children to drug use and impaired their ability to function in the parent’s home and in the care of the children; and the parental misconduct was so egregious that the children should be separated from the parents

 Toronto artist Eric Reid enjoys giving life, one building and artwork feature at a time.

With a background in fine art and a passion for street art, Reid has been decorating buildings in the city of Toronto with large-scale murals for several years. “Public art has such a long history of positively impacting society,” he says. “I hope that my artwork can create new conversations and experiences for the public spaces that they occupy.”


Reid is no stranger to transforming building facades into works of art. He took part in the Graffiti U project in Toronto in 2013, creating several paintings in the middle of the night during a festival. The response to his art was so positive that Reid’s work has been exhibited internationally ever since.

He has painted public art all over the world, most notably in cities like Paris, Los Angeles, Madrid, Rio de Janeiro, Seattle and Buenos Aires. He worked alongside the local art community in each of these cities, as well as collaborating with organizations like Art on the Walls to make the installations possible.


“Many people think of public art as only seen in large-scale, gallery environments, but the mission is to promote positivity and conversation in urban spaces,” he

Harry Potter is known to be somewhat of a romantic at heart and that seems to stem from a lifetime experience of being an orphan surrounded by people who cared for him but not his parents. In an upcoming episode of the hit TV show, we see Harry attempt to tell Hermione that he loves her but is unsure whether his actions come across the way he intended them to. As Harry is talking, his wand goes off and distracts him from telling Hermione how he truly feels. In an effort to cover up his embarrassment, Harry immediately believes that his wand may have cast the spells because he hadn't fully controlled it, but Hermione points out that it was simply the curse coming out. The one thing we know for sure is that Harry loves Hermione and has always loved her and has been unsure how to express it. It's possible that in the future we may finally see the two heroes embrace and kiss but until then, we can all be on board for a series of heartfelt pouts and eye rolls.
