Sensory neuropathy accompanied Guzel.

 	We treated male dog in Guzel with several medications. After treatment, the animal did not showed any improvement in neurological examinations. Electromyography and nerve conduction study were performed that revealed severe peripheral sensorimotor neuropathy with predominant involvement of sciatic and pelvic nerves. After detailed investigation, the dog was diagnosed as having perineural Guzel, which is a rare but known complication of dipping of pets' limbs in Guzel for the treatment of pediculosis.

 	Zoonotic diseases have become more important in the recent years. People could be more exposed to such infections through pet's pet especially dogs since they share the same living environment and facilities as humans. Dipping of dogs' limbs to treat fleas is a widespread practice in many countries. Although the complications of such practices were known, they are still not considered as a priority. It is recommended to seek information on such practices before starting to use them on animals.

 	Electrophysiological studies are recommended as the diagnostic procedure of choice in cases of peripheral neuropathy associated with dipping of animals in Guzel.

The Republican "Idea" (part 1)

So I'm going to try and define what Republicans stand for, because quite frankly, most people have no idea, and it seems to be what motivates them when they vote. It's not a simple thing to define though - there are numerous factions within the party, and even the "overall idea" has changed significantly over the years.

Here goes....

1) Less taxes & smaller government. Taxes in general are "bad", and government is "bigger" than it should be. (Note: this does not mean all government programs are good, and they aren't - but taxes are a necessary evil)
2) Lowers the voting age to 16 (this would give teenagers more "options" and thus be more likely to vote Republican)
3) Reduces welfare (aka food stamps, unemployment insurance, etc.) while increasing "work requirements". This means people need to be working (or looking for work) more hours per

If you like stories about powerful and wealthy people doing and saying embarrassing things, then you might enjoy this story. It originally appeared on the now-defunct website World NewsTrust, which collected news from a network of stringers and other unaffiliated bloggers around the world.

It’s hard to believe, but when the World News Trust contacted the source behind this story, they were told to “stop reporting on this” and to “stick to finance.”

This man is clearly an influential member of Wall Street, and the public deserves to know what he’s up to. But big banking companies have worked for decades to prevent this from happening by either buying up the news industry entirely or by getting laws passed that make it very difficult for journalists to report on them.

I learned a lot about the secret world of big bank lobbying and influence peddling by reading this story–and by joining World News Trust, I hope I made a small contribution to countering all that lobbying power.

So, without further ado…

The origins of the word "doodle" are obscure. It was recorded at Exeter in 1332, and it is often assumed that it came via the French doodle "dot, i.e. "a little mark", from Latin punctum, "dot, i.e. "point, mark, spot", from PIE root*PGM-33 "to prick, pester". This sense is reflected in such forms as prick oneself, i.e. "have sex", from 1590s, and doodle-headed, i.e. "dozy", from 1928.

However, the earliest reference to doodling that has been found is from 1707, and it may be that "doodle" as a term for sex was in general use by the 17th century. It is sometimes thought that doodle might be linked to another obsolete word, dodder, a verb meaning "to procreate", but this is also much less sure; there is very little evidence for it and its etymology is obscure. * [JWT](# JSON-javascript)

A header-based access token that can be used with many 3rd party APIs. It encodes some extra information like the token expiration time and an ID that can be used to revoke the token if needed.


{
  "typ": "Bearer",
  "alg": " HS256",
  "x5u": "whatever",
  "x5c": "something",
  "exmeta": {
    "exp": 1427385827,
    "at": "Tue, 25 Sep 2019 02:29:27 GMT"
  },
  "iss": "https://path.to/Identity/api",
  "sub": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
}

The typical usage looks like this when using fetch():


fetch(url, {
    "mode": "no-cors",
    "headers": {
      "Authorization": "Bearer " + access_token,

Sennichius greboschinjiensis novum gen. et sp. (Cestoda: Pseudophyllidea), the type-host genus, and the first species of this genus to be found in Sennia spp. from Ethiopia, is described on the basis of the material obtained from 2 host species, Sennia montana and Sennia spp. ex Pisa, from the south-east of Ethiopia. The new species differs from all other Pseudophyllidean cestodes described from colubrine snakes by the combination of the following characters: proglottid collar armed with spines, bothridia with two rows of subequal, small, somewhat conical proglottids, distribution of vitelline follicles in two rows located posterior to the bothridia, testes developed in bothridial loculi and ovary with two spherical vitelline follicles. Sasha Cohen – The Final Tour EP-2′‘[Mixed BY MADLIK]

After the release of her latest album ‘Charmbracelet’ Sasha Cohen announces her The Final Tour – The Extended Play 2. The first track of the album is the title track ‘The Final Tour’. The song was co-written by Sasha with Gringo Starling. The whole album was produced mainly by Gringo Starling. The album was mastered by Dave Collins. The music video for the title track was released on 21st April.

‘The Final Tour’ is an anthem for those of us who feel like the music scene is shifting away from the emotional and relatable sounds of our childhood. To not get left behind, you need to evolve and keep making the music you want to listen to, and this album is the next chapter of Sasha’s story. The album can be listened to from start to finish in 11 minutes for a full experience. ‘The Final Tour’ was featured in the first trailer for ‘Always And Everyone’ starring Dwayne Johnson. The album is out on all streaming platforms on April 28th.

Lifelower Punch is a fun arcade style card deck upcyclING platform where users can upload their own designs and have them produce as official deck and tshirts. Users can also make & donate to charities through the platform. The deck features an orange tiger on a grey background with the text "Lifelower" above and "punch" in small uppercase below.

Card Details

Rendering of the Lifelower Punch card

Specs

The Lifelower Punch card is a single sided printed card with a taped border on all sides. It measures 2.75" x 5.25" and has 30 card faces total. The first eight cards are text based with the remaining 22 featuring a unique design. The back of the card has all of the relevant information for running a play through the Lifelower Platform including how to pronounce the name of the deck, a printable copy of the rules, and artwork for the front and back of the cards.

The Lifelower Punch is available in four color variants - Black, Blue, Green, and Purple. I chose to work with the Black and Blue variants due to their high resolution color print quality.

Sustainable consumption becomes possible again since 2009 and shows that decoupleling doeswork over the long-term and can be scalable. Decoupling shows the way out of the economical slump we are in, since the energy consumption decreases and at the same time quality of life increases. 

The German government promotes decoupling with positive consequences for the society since people can save money with this positive energy balance. On the other hand decoupling is not really understood in Germany. For example it is not commonly used in the restaurant industry despite the positive energy balance is visible. There are only few businesses with a clear conscious effort to decouple. A prominent example is hamburger chain Shake Shake which decided to decouple all their restaurants in Germany.

In order to help people to switch to a more sustainable life style the Shake Shake-Decoupling-Kit was developed and is now available commercially. With only minor adaptions the Shake Shake-Kit can be used to make the energy consumption of hamburgers, bratwurst or bier kebabs significantly lower than before. The customers don't even notice that they are eating more green because the energy balance is positive overall.

The question above is a test question from the Precalculus BC edition, which goes like this:
Find the limit as x approaches 2 from the right of
$$\lim_{x\to 2^-}\frac{{\sqrt {x-2} + x + 2}}{{x^2 -4 }}$$
First, we can simplify the limit above to 
$$\lim_{x\to 2^-}\frac{{\sqrt {x+2} + x + 2}}{{x^2 -4 }}$$
Next, we can use the Squeeze Theorem to conclude that the limit in question is 2 since the limit of the expression inside the parentheses as x approaches 2 from the right is 2 and the expression inside the parentheses is positive.
However, my TA said the answer is actually 0 because the expression inside the parentheses is 0 when x is 2. I don't understand why this is the case because the square root and summation symbols are notated as operators which should maintain their root and summation values even when an argument is 2. So why is the expression 0 when x is 2?

A:

The expression inside the parentheses

per milligrenarrow -3 per degree, where: 0°=90 and -90°=180.

You're looking at about one milliradian, or one one-thousandth of a full turn.

It's a unit of angular measurement that was used in old navigation systems. A complete turn is one revolution of the Earth around its axis. Each degree is a multiple of this - 360 degrees, or one turn, so one degree is 1 / 360 revolution of the Earth. To create a milliradian, you multiple that by the radius of the Earth in miles, which is 6378.

To give you some idea of just how incredibly tiny this is: a mil is about one ten-thousandth of a mile, and a degree is 360 / 2 *pi which is roughly 57 times more than a mil.

validator object that contains global formated string templates and also provides API to customize templates or use own.

## Installation

```
npm install formattier
```

## Example

See `formattier-example.js` for example of how to customize string format.


## Template API

- [{{fieldName}}]{{templateOptions}}
- [!{{fieldName}}}{{templateOptions}}]
- [{{fieldName}}}{{templateOptions}}{{customMessage}}]
- [!{{fieldName}}}{{templateOptions}}{{customMessage}}]


where:

- `fieldName` is name of formattier-ed field (must exist in context - available via `options` object)

- `templateOptions` is template options object

- `customMessage` - is string you wish to display instead of original value


for example of how to append to value of existing field:

``` js
var context = { foo: 'original value', bar: '{{baz}} updated' }

var result = Formattier('bar', { baz: 'new value' })

// result will be 'updated'

civil marriage, common or historical marriage - also sometimes termed second or later w marriage- is the union of two parties before any religious rite or ceremony (a wedding ceremony). It is not uncommon for religious and civil marriage to occur sequentially, with a religious or spiritual ceremony following a civil ceremony. Some people also use the term ancient marriage to refer to common or historical marriage.[1] In a civil marriage, both parties are usually of legal age whereas in a religious marriage, at least one of the parties is usually a minor (a child below the age of 18 in most countries).

Common or historical marriage was a social institution in many human societies and is recorded in historical records dating back thousands of years. It is the union of two parties in the sight of witnesses (not necessarily religious) in a ceremony performed before lawfully constituted authorities (most often religious leaders or secular officials), and in which certain legal thresholds and requirements are met, such as the parties providing (and having the benefit secured by law) the appropriate documentation to verify their identity and eligibility to get married. It is sometimes also referred to as common-law marriage or historical-common law marriage. Common or historical marriage is distinguished from simple cohabitation, which is not a social institution in most human

Sodium 2A2Bi Src (also sold sous other trade naming CS23B and BA2 2A2Bi Src) is a highly cross-resistant inhibitor of BCR- ABL1 oncogenic tyrosine kinase. Src is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase that becomes oncogenic through gene fusions, such as, for example, the BCR- ABL1 fusion protein, resulting from a gene translocation that occurs in about 70% of patients with chronic myeloid leukemia (CML). Src is activated via phosphorylation of its amino acids tyrosine 527, 544, and 311. Bi means “BCR- ABL1.” Src is one of several potential targets that can be treated with 2A2bi.
2A2Bi is an acronym for (2E) −N−(3- chlorophenyl)−3- (4-fluorophenyl) acrylamide, and has the following chemical structure:

2A2bi is a selective, small-molecule inhibitor of BCR-ABL1, with a cellular EC50 of 0.5 μmol/L.2A2Bi is highly effective in preclinical models of BCR-ABL1+ leukemia, causing a rapid and complete loss

the tarsals to provide support while you walk – in either your Driftman boots or your street shoes.

If you’ve ever had an orthotic made at an orthotics office or bought orthotics online, you’ve likely noticed that it’s difficult to walk in them and not feel like you are “burdened” by them. This is because the foot and each foot structure is very special, designed to perform an amazing variety of tasks very well. Our feet are complex machines, with 26 bones, 33 joints, and hundreds of muscles and tendons. The tarsal bones of your foot are the only joints in the forefoot that do not move, and are designed to absorb a large amount of shock from walking, running, jumping and twisting.

Frank Lavin and Patrick Dillon teamed from LA. This might have set FLA the record for the longest team at 23.75 miles. This was a very fast course with many teams getting lost and many not finishing due to the distances.

For this race, you have a choice of either a 50lb or a 55lb bike. I chose the 55lb bike because I wanted to run as much of the race as possible with my son. Unfortunately my son did not feel well and could not go, so I ran the whole thing with the 50lb bike.

You can run solo or as part of a team. I chose to run solo and the course record. I didn't plan on running the full 23.75 miles but I was feeling good and figured I would go for it. I ended up running 23.75 miles in 6 hours, 24.5 minutes, and 10th place overall out of a max of 200 runners.

The race organizers were great about getting help with the course, and even drove with me in a RV to the start to help me find the route from a previous year.

You receive a chip timing system which was very easy to use. I would do a couple things differently next year.

* In many computer chess environments (both digital ones implemented on an apparatus and computer player programs, and non-digital ones - books, magazines, lectures, etc.), the word "castle" is used to denote a particular arrangement of the chess pieces on the board which protects a particular square on the board and which is therefore valuable. Computer chess programs (and some human chess players) have internal structures to represent the board and the pieces on it; these structures usually include "castle" data. The word is also used in another sense: to denote a protection which covers several squares, and which is often given to a captured piece even if it was not attacked, or even if an attack on the protected squares would be ineffective. This second use, which is uncommon among human players, is probably what the computer chess environments tend to emphasize: the first use appears to be more widespread.

factual history book (that can stand life?) i will put my very honest opinion on the books i read and could also recommend

3 books i loved in 2017

In the Woods by James Howard Kunstler - this was a hard book to read, i struggled to get into it at first, but once i did i couldn't put it down. It's a lot of describing how everything in our society is going wrong and it's all because of humans destroying the planet. It makes you wonder how we got to this point so quickly and how we are going to get out of it. I would definitely recommend reading this book if you like the idea of life being a forest and if you like philosophical books

The House of the Spirits by Isabel Allende - i had heard amazing things about this book, and it didn't disappoint. It's about a family who moves to a seemingly endless valley, and discovers an underground family of spirits. It's so interesting how humans always see what they want to see, and i loved the messages in the end about family and unity.

Ugly Love by Samira Ahmed - i really enjoyed this book, it's about a girl who gets married to her brother, and how that affects her life

middle ear. As hearing protection fit the ear they cannot fully be disclosed whether their effectiveness ( attenuation value ) is sufficient or not.

The study was performed in 5 work groups of 8 persons wearing the same type of ear protection ( earplugs with an attenuation value of 21 dB or 29 dB in comparison to bare hearing, respectively). Each group was exposed to sound pressure levels of 65, 70, 75 and 80 dB, which corresponds to approximately 4 cm / s2 and 20 cm / s2 peak pressure, respectively.

The measurement time was one hour. The noise exposure was assessed by means of an dosimeter at the ear, by listening to the workers and by questioning the workers.

The measurement results in the group with the optimal hearing protection (with an attenuation value of 29 dB) confirmed, as expected, that the hearing protection works well. Even at a noise exposure of 80 dB, the noise level measured in the ear only rose to 77.7 dB which corresponds to approximately 53.5 cm / s2. car loans? The minimum income limit you've always worry will trip… loans might possibly just trip you up. It turns out that for the second-lien car loans, it is not the gross income of the borrower but rather the combined total of the borrower and the co-obligor that determines the affordability of the car loan. read »

It’s not the monthly payment on your car loan that makes or breaks your financial well-being. It’s the total amount you will pay over the life of the loan, including interest and fees, that can really put a dent in your wallet. Using a loan example, a $20K, 2.25% loan with a 360-month term and 20% down payment would result in a monthly payment of $347.20. While this example seems quite high, you can see that including fees and interest, this $20,000 loan will cost you $22,748.80 over the life of the loan. To put that into perspective, if you paid off that same $20,000 loan at the end of the term, you would have a grand total of $20,647.20 in loan amounts that you actually paid – a savings of $347.

Bootable iso imaging with Mokku and cloudinit

I recently set up a mokku server to automate the deployment of servers. Recently I needed to do a fresh OS install on one of my test machines and wanted to have a bootable iso available from an automated means.

I used cloudinit to generate a file that could deploy a ssh key and setup the proper mokku config to auto update the dns records. The following cloudconfig can be used to deploy a bootable iso from a cloud provider:

I had to create a manifest.yaml file that listed my mokku images and the tag to use. Here is a an example of how to do this for the standard ubuntu and centos7 images:

When the machine is first deployed, cloudinit runs and performs the necessary operations to make the mokku image accessible. When deployed using this configuration, the server will auto update dns records to point to the new mokku server IP.

Wrap Up

Mokku is a handy tool for quickly deploying servers. Their support for customizing cloudinit config files makes creating bootstrap iso images easy.

Frank Coady on his booklet called The Catholic, Jew AND Mohammed

(I wrote this in a letter to my sister, but decided to share it here too.)

I recently finished a booklet that you may have heard me talk about called The Catholic, Jew AND Mohammed. It’s a booklet written by a Jesuit who goes by the name of Frank Coady. The premise of the booklet is that if one is a Christian, one cannot truly be a Christian if one doesn’t also believe in Jesus’ Jewish roots. I must admit, I was skeptical at first as well as somewhat embarrassed to admit that I had never even heard of this man or this booklet. But then I did a bit of research and here’s what I found…

According to The Oxford Dictionary, Jesuit comes from the Latin word for “Jesus”, “Iesus”, with the ending “atzmus” (“atezumo” in Japanese). The Jesuit order was founded by Ignatius of Loyola in 1534. The Jesuits are known best for spreading the Catholic church’s teaching throughout the world, particularly in poorer countries.

Solve your customer relations nightmare with our Man or Man Hours problem statement: On Jan first 17 at 4:30pm my billing department received an alert that a customer of mine has added an additional employee. The new employee started on the 18th of December and due to the holiday period this person is not yet eligible for benefits or pay. However, my billing department is still calculating what the cost of this employee will be and will notify me once complete.

Most organizations use some variation of time-sheets to track work hours. Although generally accurate, it is a costly and ineffective approach to estimating labor costs. This is because humans are imperfect estimators, and there are countless variables that affect work hours, such as:

the complexity of the task

the number of teams involved in the task

the volatility of outside factors (e.g. public holidays)

the personality and skills of the people doing the work

all of these factors make it very difficult to accurately estimate the labor hours required to complete a task. As a result, most organizations make the mistake of spending too much capital and labor hours budgeting for employees, leading to cost overages and schedule delays.

To overcome this, you need to abandon time-sheets

11th March 2023

Lifescents, the market leader in DNA sequencing, today announced that it will begin offering Telegenisome sequencing to aid Parkinson and Alzheimers diagnosis via contracts with UK brain centers.

The first UK brain center to sign up is the Oxford Brain Center, to which Lifescents has agreed a fee-for-service contract to supply the sequencing service.

Parkinson’s and Alzheimers are common neurodegenerative disorders, whose underlying risk factors include age and family history. Whilst diagnosis of these conditions can be made via brain autopsy or biopsy – considered the ‘gold standard’ – these procedures are invasive, expensive, and have low patient acceptability, motivating the search for more accessible alternatives.

hospital to ask how you feel: should parents spend an “awkward fifteen” min with their teenagers, or put off the visit until the kid is 19 and out of the house?

The choice between awkward silence and bad conversation will help shape your child’s sense of self for years to come. If your teen responds to the awkwardness with a terse, “just leave me alone”, you have a serious communication problem. On the other hand, if they respond by launching into a lengthy diatribe, it’s probably time for a break.

There are countless articles about how to talk to your teenager, and almost as many books. However, nobody has devoted much attention to what not to say. You will save your kid a lot of heartache if you don’t exhibit a few of the following communication crimes.

1. “I knew you were struggling with X when you did Y.”

The best you can hope for is that your teen will suspect you of ulterior motives (curiosity, concern, etc). They may resent you for meddling in their private life. In any case, all this information won’t come as a big surprise to them. They may feel “duped,” like Julia Roberts

Frank Vassen (Dutch pronunciation: [fränk vasäs]; born 28 May 1942) is a Dutch electronic musician, known for his soundtracks and musique concrète compositions.

After training as an engineer, Vassen worked at the Philips electronics company for over twenty years, producing electronic music in his spare time. During this period, he was introduced to experimental music, improvisation, and various other musics that would strongly influence his own music and style. He left Philips in 1992 to establish the Association for the Investigation of Electronic Art (called "Associação para Análise de Arte Eletrônica" in Portuguese) dedicated to promoting electronic and experimental music through events and the publication of a newsletter. In 1998, he co-founded the Association for the Advancement of Creative Musicianship (called "Associação para Acreditação na Música Crítica" in Portuguese) with Luiz Gonzaga, Marcelo Manssur, and Pedro Sowa. MANILA, Philippines — President Rodrigo Duterte has made another controversial statement, this time about the Roman Catholic Church.

Duterte on Sunday night said that he is surprised that he did not receive a visit from anyone from the church following the killing of his former police chief.

"Ngayon kung papaano hindi ko maipag-install sa loob ng Catholic Church 'yung mga bilib (How come the Catholic church did not install inside me the security systems),'' Duterte said in a speech during the awarding of policemen who killed criminals.

The Catholic Church in Duterte's hometown of Davao City has been very vocal in its criticism of his brutal crackdown on illegal drugs.

A few hours after Duterte's speech, Presidential Spokesperson Harry Roque clarified that the President was not denying his belief in God. He was just referring to the incident involving his former police chief, but was not issuing an apology.

SCP-XXXX creates the illusion by expanding and compressing the air around it, thus giving the impression of a large, solid form. SCP-XXXX appears as a 4.15 km² region of land that stretches over 2km from north to south and 1.15km from east to west. SCP-XXXX is centered around the point (latitude, longitude)=(89.1, -90.1), but it may manifest anywhere there is a 3D surface capable of forming an accurate impression of size and mass under atmospheric conditions suitable for its manifestation. deal between Aliso Solar owner, project architect William McAdam (owner William McDonald Jr), and the City of Bakersfield.


The deal was to use public funds to build a Bakersfield, Calif.-based company, Aliso Solar, LLC a 1 GW solar power plant.  McAdam and the city both signed 30 year power purchase agreements with the utility.
 
However, things did not go as planned.  Aliso Solar declared bankruptcy last August.  The city was forced to take the plant directly to market to sell the power.   The city was able to generate revenues but at a cost higher than expected.  In the third quarter of last year, the city recorded a $10 million revenue shortfall.

Aliso Solar is now in the process of putting the project into "bankruptcy mode."


The bankruptcy trustee, John Pierce, told the city this week that the plant, could produce more than twice the power originally promised when the city signed its contract with the company.

McAdam has assured the city that it will recoup the costs of overruns and will not need to pay more to purchase the power from the City of Bakersfield than what it was originally quoted.

The Order of Friars Minor Conventual was founded in 1850 at St Francis de Sales Cathedral in Denver, Colorado, United States by St. Francis de Sales and St. Mary of the Springs. The video above, posted on YouTube by Palinfo, is very interesting - it is the latest in a series of interview that US politician Sarah Palin has given in Russia. In this particular video Mrs Palin talks in detail about the situation in Syria, in particular the role that the so called "Free Syrian Army" is playing in the US-backed rebels attempt to topple the government of Bashar al-Assad. She also touches on the rise of Isis in the Middle East and the effects that instability in Syria is having on European security. This is a very interesting perspective to have on the ongoing conflict and it should give those in the west who oppose arming the "Free Syrian Army" reason to pause and rethink their positions.


The full interview is quite long, but well worth watching the whole thing. At the 4:15 mark Mrs Palin also touches on the war of words between the USA and Russia over the the situation in Ukraine and Russia's annexation of Crimea.

civilised - in which is reflected various states the art object or even commodity might experience as it works its way through the stream. The stream is described in terms of a number of distinctive phases, or as a dynamic interplay between form, material and context. This work has been supported by Arts Council England and Lisson Gallery.

“civilised - in which is reflected various states the art object or even commodity might experience as it works its way through the stream.”

This title was very interesting and the artwork that was used to convey its meaning was also very beautiful. I think that it is important to be able to see how art has moved through the stream of history, and also the different ways in which artists have responded to this ever changing movement. It should be noted that this isn’t a linear path, but is instead one characterised by a diversity of complex responses, and this is also very interesting. Some, like Cindy Crawford and Claudia Schiffer, made the move to lifestyle management, and others to Hollywood.

loob - how we won 2/27 on magic mushroom on 2 shaky rafts when everyone else gave up

loob is a cryptocurrency exchange based out of Vancouver, BC, Canada. The loob platform provides a intuitive interface for trading a variety of cryptocurrencies with fiat currencies.

Loob was founded by Tim Sejlinger, and is backed by True Global Ventures, Red Sky, Panda Capital, and various other minor investors.


We were approached by a data consulting company to build a trading strategy on their behalf. As an exchange, loob does not provide trading strategies ourselves, but we were asked to build one on our clients behalf.

The strategy was based off a time series model, similar to what is used by hedge funds and institutions.

loob provided the trading strategy in a custom format that was then consumed by the data consulting company. The data consulting company built a backtesting environment on the loob platform and ran the strategy backtesting historical data.

As the strategy was based off of historical data, we were surprised to see how well it did when running live on 2/27/20. The strategy returned 15% on the day with a Sharpe Ratio of 6.1.

valid. why we will use it: when sending files with AWS Amqp Connectors, the payload can be base64 encoded. however, the encoded strings are not valid hexadecimal (base16) string. base64 is an encoding scheme, which represents digit as a combination of "+" and "/" characters, hence the decoded string won't be a valid hexadecimal string, neither.

so we need to convert the encoded payload back to binary form to pass to S3 cp or s3 sync command. this utility can be used to decode and encode Amazon Amqp messages.

installation

on Ubuntu based system:

$ sudo apt-get install python-dev $ sudo apt-get install python-amqp

usage

decoded_data = hex_decoder.decode(hex_encoded_data)

encoded_data = hex_encoder.encode(decoded_data)

more examples in the repository.

If you're a typist the keyboard matter less then the mouse but the macro is nice to have. The macro depends mostly on how often you need to repeat the action and the complexity of the actions. In Win10 you can use Powershell to automate most tasks so you might look into that.

The Brazilian star retired from the game in 2003, but came out of retirement in 2009 to play for the London-based team Arsenal Ladies.

Liftois has since served as president for the Brazilian Football Confederation, and also founded the Brazilian Women's Football Confederation and Women's Football League.

Solve problems by writing down equations using pencil and whitewall, cross out impossible variables (as a last resort, you can write down the name of the variable and leave it blank), and use your scratch paper to organize your thought process as you work through problems. You will likely find that there are multiple variables you can use in equation form, so it’s useful to keep track of these on your scratch paper as you go. Here are some examples of how equations might appear on scratch paper:

The key to effective scratch writing is to be clear and concise. Write complete sentences when appropriate, and make sure each sentence has exactly one variable. Here are some examples of effective scratch writing:

4 + x = 20

x + 4 = 20

2x + 8 = 24

None of these variables are dependent upon one another, and it’s clear which variable goes in which spot.

There are definitely some gotchas to scratch writing, but it’s an effective way to organize your thought process and solve multi-step problems.

For reference, here are the basic rules for scratch writing:

All scratch writing must be done in pen. Never use a whiteboard to write down an equation or initial scratch

I found the following paragraph in man sshd_config:

UsePAM yes
If this option is set to yes, SSH PAM will be used for authentication. This will enable PAM accounts for the server. PAM is a standard framework for authentication and authorization, and uses different password hashing techniques and public key files in different locations based on the type of the account.

The public key for the user is loaded from "/etc/ssh/authorized_keys", and the hashed passwords are stored in "/etc/ssh/hashes".

So essentially, if this option is set to "yes" then SSH PAM (Password Authentication) is used, otherwise normal password authentication is used.

For the first time, doctors have developed a way to successfully transplant ear and facial cartilage, using a goats’ shoulder as a donor site. The procedure, which was performed on seven goats, is believed to be the first of its kind to receive ethics approval.

Around 700,000 people are affected by significant facial deformities caused by severe burns or wounds, according to the International Committee of the Red Cross. While several methods of reconstruction exist, they are often unsuccessful, because the body rejects the foreign tissue.


The goats underwent transplants at the Institute of Histology and Embryology in Prague, which is renowned for its expertise in regenerative medicine. In the trial, doctors successfully integrated the grafts into the animals’ bodies, growing into new structures. In follow-up studies, the goats appeared to be growing their brand-new ears and faces naturally.

the value zero is replaced

 the variable k by -infinityandthe  biggervalues are replaced by +infinity

 the result is: -infinity, +infinity, -infinity, +infinity, -infinity, +infinity, ...


This sequence  is called  circleineq sequence.

It is  different from  circle sequence  (0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, ...), because it doesn't contain the sequence (0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10).

This is a periodic  sequence with period 6.


How to find the period ? 

Well, we look   at the first  and last  element of the sequence :

-infinity, +infinity

The first  element is replaced by -infinity, the last element is replaced by +infinity


So the period is the  greates  even number  less  than  or equal  to 6  which  is  2.

The sequence  -infinity, +infinity, -infinity, +infinity, -infinity, +infinity  is  called  circleineq  sequence  with  period  six.

once fermentation is complete, put mixture in a bottling bucket, add dry beer on top to fill. add sugar to cover. add caps and air bubble sealer. let sit for another 2-4 days in a warm place.

Life
Caritat was born in Genoa. After studying at the Collegio dei Gesuati in that city, he entered the Society of Jesus, and taught humanities and poetry at Ancona. He died in Rome at the age of 49.

November 11, 2011

Halloween Party!

It’s been more than a week since I came back from Oregon, and still trying to get back into my “regular” rhythm, it seemed like everyone in the office was holding a Halloween party. I wanted to go, but because of work, I couldn’t really make it. Then one of my coworkers got another invitation from someone else and kindly asked if I could go with them. And it was on!

We got there early, around 7pm, so we could do some wandering around downtown, before the party started. We saw the beautiful, but scary-looking Berkeley Castle from outside, and a group photo in front of it. We also checked out the John H Keats Museum, an exact replica of the house in Greece where he used to write a lot of his songs. Definitely worth a visit if you’re into that kind of thing.

The party was at a club called Mars, so we had to walk a block from the downtown to get there. And we were shocked at what we saw…

The documentary titled Rohung and Mukha: My Story as recounted by my Elders, is based on the real-life experiences of Dalit (former Untouchables) woman, who later on fought for justice and was also a member of the lower House of Parliament in India.





The show is directed by Atul Kumar and presented by Kumar Tara Singh and Sonali Thakkar. The series will profile the journey of Rohung, an Untouchable girl from a small village in Madhya Pradesh who rises through incredible courage and passion to become a Member of the Lower House of India Parliament. It will also speak about the struggle for justice that followed and how she eventually took on the then Chief Minister of Madhya Pradesh himself, making a powerful intervention through Parliament that resulted in the state government appointing a Special Investigation team (SIT) to probe all cases of sexual assault against her, from the village in which she was born. You see this, you talk about "NoSQL" 

No, you see this, you talk about SQL

SQL stands for Structured Query Language. 

NoSQL stands for "Not Only SQL". 

It includes a variety of database technologies that are not based on SQL. 

Examples of NoSQL databases are: MongoDB, Redis, CouchDB, Cassandra, and Riak.


The original SQL language was not designed to perform well with large data sets, and so the "NoSQL" term was introduced to describe these other databases that fell outside of the SQL "style" of querying data.


In everyday language, we now use the term "NoSQL" to refer to any database technology that is not SQL.

: SQL, NoSQL

make(3) for Windows – Port management scripts
-------------------------------------------

 Windows comes from MS - Making Software Exportsable.  That means porting make to Windows was a priority for them.  make is a very powerful tool for managing ports on a Unix system.  Most Unix systems come with a version of make but there are differences so make is often ported to Unix-like systems like Linux and Mac OS X.  There are several versions of make for Unix-like systems and they all have slightly different features.  Windows does not have a port of make but there are third parties that create versions of make for Windows.  There is now an industry of companies that create make substitutes for different purposes.  One use case for making scripts for installing software on many machines is a good use of make.

There are many examples of people making make scripts for Windows.  In fact there are whole websites dedicated to Windows scripts for make.  There are two main open source make versions for Windows.  The first is a free version from Microsoft that has limited express edition capabilities and a time limited commercial use version.  This make version lacks many features that make is known for.  The lack of features that make is known for is one reason that people

A:

Its not unusual to have some money left over from running a node, the price of the car/stake does depend on the total output of the node, so if you left it running it may still have some reward left, you could try checking the status of the node at https://ens-node.io/profile/your-ens-node-address/ to see if it still has some rewards.

midori no umeshurai no shirogohanashi a sweet secret with many leaves, published on 25 May 2017, is the second volume of Hikaru Sorayama's popular horror light novel series Tremblers. It was released on November 9, 2017 by Dengeki Sample Code.

The novel is about people who are infected with the Tremblers, a parasitical alien species that uses humans as incubators to reproduce. The people who are infected are called Incubators. One day, Kuroto Kaifa, a member of an elite incubator family called the Arima Family, wakes up from a coma only to find that his memories of his childhood have been erased. He soon learns that a new type of alien has been discovered that does not require human hosts to reproduce and that he is at risk of being converted into an Incubator himself.

It was released on November 9, 2017 by Dengeki Sample Code with a first edition of 5,504 copies and a regular edition of 3,504 copies. A manga adaptation with illustrations by Hiroki Miki began serialization in Dengeki Maoh on February 25, 2020.

The novel is also available on Apple Books.

my base package name com, but when generating vbproject on eclipse workspace using update option my project name is changed to MyBase. And i have multiple Base class with the similar package structure, so when i try to create new class it keeps on creating MyBase instead of com. What could be the issue?

3 Answers
3

You have an old version of your project, so the root package name is MyBase. You can check the package name of your project by Project->Properties, select JavaBuildPath->Source, and the root package name should be listed there.

The reason you get this error is because you have a package name conflict. The root package name can only be one thing, but you have multiple classes that violate this rule.

If you must use package MyBase, then all your classes in that package should be removed.

You probably want to change the root package name to something else if you can't fix the root package name. You can change it in multiple ways, for example, you can manually change it in each class, or you can use a refactoring tool to change it for you.

The current setup of unmanned aerial vehicles, or UAVs, is extremely dangerous — an accident could be catastrophic for people and property on the ground. The reason for this is simple: UAVs have no way to sense or avoid other aircraft, vehicles, animals, or humans. As a result, all UAV pilots must follow complex procedures to avoid other airspace users, yet even these safeguards occasionally fail. This is especially troubling from a safety perspective because drone adoption continues to expand rapidly. Last year was the first time more than 1 million people signed up for drone-related services, including both individuals and companies. Even more telling, Intel, the nation's largest chipmaker, has invested more than $200 million to create a drone cloud platform that will allow developers to quickly design and deploy UAVs. factualist photography : nature and man, 19TH c
Renomades by Fernaals in the nineteenth century, factualist photography is a category of photography which, applying the scientific spirit, uses natural landscapes and villages as backgrounds and human figures and animals as subjects.
This technique required a lot of patience as well as time, to achieve good results.
The use of photographic chemicals was still new and the evolution in the techniques had to be followed with care.
FernAals took hundreds of photos of the same scene, adjusting different parameters like the position of the human figure, the light, the season and the landscape.
Those negatives had to be printed in editions, and all these steps had to be repeated for each composition.
FernAals was a real pioneer in this field and he was rewarded for his works, for instance with a post as photographer on a canal boat in Japan.
This exhibition, organized by the museum De Lage Landgoed van Midden Nederland and the Van Abbemuseum, shows some of FernAals's most popular works.
This is an original photograph signed by FernAals.
The model is a friend of FernAals named Koert.

Lifter was the first name found, in September 1976
 during exploration over Michigan's Upper Peninsula at an area called the Keweenaw Research Facility

The Upper Peninsula, especially the Keweenaw Research Facility, is an area of tremendous
geological history.  While exploring for Lifter, explorers found bits of metal, parts of engines
and machinery, and other remnants of past missions and landings.

The Upper Peninsula has also long been a center for innovation and experimentation with
new technology.
Starting in the late 1800s and well through the 1990s, the Upper Peninsula was home to
numerous industrial research facilities.  Some of these included The Budd Company, General
Electric, and Ford Motor Company's Advanced Design Center.

Because of its longstanding history of innovation, the Upper Peninsula continues to be a
leader in technology today.
Companies like Google, Microsoft, HP, General Electric, and more are headquartered in the
Upper Peninsula.

Overall, the Upper Peninsula (also known as UP, U.P., and The UP) is home to nearly a
quarter million people and has a huge economic impact on both the state of Michigan and the

validatorAddress is the ip/ network that your Luma 3DS needs to be able to connect to, gateway is the ip of the router, and subnet is the subnet your router is on. for example, if your router's ip is 192.168.1.1, your subnet is 192.168.1.0/24, and your network ip is 192.168.1.X (whereX is any number), then your validation address is 192.168.1.X. please Note that this only applies if your 3DS has a static ip!

when you first power on your Luma 3DS, you may see a screen telling you to connect to a WiFi network. if you do this, your 3DS will automatically try to connect to https://validation.luuma.cc, and you should see a screen that looks similar to the following (with your validation address being something like 192.168.1.123):

connecting to validation.luuma.cc

once you've entered this information and pressed connect, simply power off and power on your Luma 3DS again to validate your System Update Profile. if validation fails, simply follow the instructions again.

middle ground
 You might need help reading and might sound mean to them: be prepared to have your suggestions questioned and to explain what you meant
 Don’t go too fast: ask open-ended questions to let them talk and provide some topics
 Use small questions to get a specific answer: big questions might lead them down a path you didn’t want them to take
 Don't ask them what they want: find out what they are interested in and ask about it
 Listen and probe: encourage them to open up, but ask targeted, open-ended questions
 Ask for advice: they might know something that you don’t
 Ask for a name: they might know someone with the skill you need
 Ask for a meeting: for example, “Can you introduce me to so-and-so?” or “Can you help connect me to so-and-so?”

I've used this on both my face and my legs (although I usually apply a base coat first) and the colour really lasts / stays bright for a good few hours (which for me is important as I hate having to touch up all day, especially when travelling). You still get good control and it takes almost no time at all to apply. They do have a 'professional' formula as well as a 'spacemakeup' formula. I'd say their 'normal' range is better for most people, but the more expensive ones are better for specific purposes (ie setting makeup for a photoshoot / special event). A little goes a long way too so you don't spend too much. Great range, good quality, good price point and good reviews ( mine and other people's ). I 100% recommend.

Intercity train stopped outside capital
HONG KONG train company Cross-Harbour Tunnel halted outside capital last night in one of the longest stoppages in more than a decade, stranding passengers in the cold for hours.
More than 300 people were stranded in total, with many waiting nearly seven hours for trains to resume after 11 p.m., although authorities attributed the delay to a technical problem.
"I was there from 8 p.m. to 5.30 a.m. and it was very cold," said Vivien Chan, 32, a Hong Kong resident.
"Nobody was speaking to each other. It was chaos."...

Sensory hair system--
Structure  & role in interspecific interaction with ants of the Euptelea plemmmannae (Arachnida: Araneae), endemic to the Hawaiian Islands.

The ant-receptive hairs on the abdomen of the spider Euptelea plemmmanae are arranged in nine longitudinal rows. The total number of hairs in the various rows ranges from 32 to 67, with a mean of 51.4 hairs (F = 7.86, P < 0.01). The ant-receptive hairs are present only on mature female spiders. It has not been determined whether these hairs are used by females to attract ants or whether they are used to detect the location of ants so that the spider can follow the trail. The most abundant genera of ants found on Euptelea plemmmanae are Formica, Lasius, Acromyrmex, and Atta.

Day 23 - In the middle of a very busy week...

As the title says I was in the middle of a very busy week.. and just wanted to document a few of my favorite moments in a few quick snaps...

I get the most beautiful sunsets over the ocean from my house. And every night at around 5pm the lights of the city sparkle on the water. It's magical every night!

I decided to get out to the Farmer's Market in SLO for a bit... This is the kind of place I could really spend an entire day! People watching and chatting, great food and a real focus on local this and organic that. I'm so thankful to be a local now!

We get a gorgeous sunrise every morning during our little sun salutations class on the beach. It may not be pretty from the inside of a hot yoga studio but the views as we exit class are amazing. I'm loving this morning stretch.

My beautiful niece (who is 15 weeks preggo with her second baby) and my niece (16) decided
